https://www.crunchyroll.com/series/GXJHM3P19/bocchi-the-rock

The subject is right in your wheelhouse: It's a light hearted series about a young musician who's developed an online following but has always wanted to join a band - having to overcome crippling social anxiety to do so. It's a short series, 12 episodes in total and only 23 minutes per episode so you could probably do 2 per posting between seasons of some of your longer run series.

structured.name: KJMx
structured.text:
[K-pop/ Indie Rock] The Rose— Sorry

The Rose’s debut single Sorry was almost never released. Melody composed by drummer Hajoon and lyrics by singer Woosung. The Rose’s label at the time wanted them to do something more upbeat, and the label also wanted them to dance like typical K-pop idols. But The Rose never gave in and they managed to debut with Sorry, written by them alone. The song took off in Europe and the Americas to everyone’s surprise. Their first tour was a world tour around Europe.

JUNE 2026: Content Requests & 🗓️SCHEDULE
Tim Welch Vocal Studio

Yesterday

Edit

Happy June Everyone!

(💥K-POP/KOREAN ARTIST REQUESTS GO 👉HERE!👈)

GRATITUDE 🙏

It's incredible to me how rewarding this new work is that I get to do with you all. As a vocal coach for so many years working with one-on-one students, I had no idea I could find something like this that could be equally as fulfilling. I feel that as much as I am educating you all with my years of being a musician and educator, you all are educating me just as much with musical worlds I've never heard of until now. I'm beyond honored to get to keep doing this with you all, and I have so much gratitude for the incredible support you all are giving me to keep this going. It is truly life-changing on many levels. I'm so excited for June and even more excited about new ideas you all come up with for content requests on this post. Thank you all so much. 🥲

💥Here's the SCHEDULE FOR THE MONTH! Expect tweaks!

📝 HOW TO REQUEST

One song or content idea per comment, add GENRE if you can, no worries if you don't know.

Example: [J-POP] or [Metal] Artist — Song Title

Then drop the link and say whatever you want about it underneath!

💥If it's a cover — put whoever's SINGING it on the first line, and mention the original artist in your notes below.

GENRE LABEL:

If it's a song or musical piece, preferably add a genre identifier to help my crew organize the post for the CONTENT REQUEST TRACKER which is NOT FULLY UPDATED as of 6/1 btw. *You could help me out by reporting any mistakes you see, there are definitely some.

PRIDE MONTH 🏳️‍🌈🏳️‍⚧️

I'm opening up to the community to help me pick one more openly LGBTQ+ artist to celebrate for my new Tim Pick Mondays. Let me know in the comments!

Thanks again, and I look forward to seeing you all in the chats!

Cheers,

Tim

Posted Yesterday at 12:53 PM

[VTuber] Tsunomaki Watame - My song

https://youtu.be/6VGkeUaX-zk?si=WfgVvSxv6rduanZ9

Watame is one of my favorite Hololive JP artists that you haven't really checked out yet. This song is both incredibly emotional yet upbeat and empowering. It is deeply personal and tells a lot about her journey, her struggles, and her perseverance. Some lyrics:

"During these trials and tribulations, can I find my own voice? Can I find my battle cry?
If I couldn’t, I would’ve given up a long time ago"

She does a lot for her community, streaming long hours, putting out tons of short content, and has released three full albums along with several other EPs and singles. One thing that stands out to me is the effort she puts into reaching the English speaking community: when she makes a big announcement, she will post in English on the Hololive subreddit, and a lot of her videos include English subtitles, not just the MVs (I really enjoy her series "Have a Nice Day", where she gets to spend time with other Hololive members in-person). 

I do hope you get to hear this and more of her; she has so much variety in music and is an incredible singer and creator

The most viewed utaite cover of all time at 168 Million views by the most influential utaite in history Mafumafu. This has insanely huge cultural impact in Japan. A super emotional song originally by the vocaloid producer Iori Kanzaki and you need to read the lyrics when you are reacting to the song it is an intense topic.
Mafumafu was a big inspiration for Ado and wrote/produced several songs for her. He is a great producer, amazing song writer and unbelievable vocalist out of this world. 
Fun fact on the side he also does a bit of vtubing.

We definitely need a 5 song deep study on Mafumafu, who you can argue is the utaite that build the foundation for what the scene has become today.

[vtuber] Ironmouse x Michi Mochievee - Daidaidaidaidaikirai [Hatsune Miku VS Kasane Teto (Cover)]
https://youtu.be/gFmrDB-99fU 

This is a cover of a vocaloid song by Amala, performed for the first Club Ironmouse concert last summer. The song is about a pair of rivals, each obsessed with trying to one up the other, in the original video one has a devil motif and the other an angel - in this version Michi wears a halo while Ironmouse's lore is that she's Satan. The camera work and choreography is pretty interesting as there's a lot is going on with the backdrop as they transition from one stage setting to another in the middle of the song.

Anime series - Fate/Zero and Fate/Stay Night

https://www.imdb.com/title/tt2051178/
https://www.imdb.com/title/tt3621796/

Even though Death Note and AoT are still a ways off from being done, I would like to recommend the Fate series as a potential next anime. Fate is probably my favorite anime to date, and has some of the best character writing I've seen. The anime was also produced by studio ufotable, who were the ones behind the recent Demon Slayer movie, and are well known as one of the best animation studios in anime. Lengthwise, the main story of Fate (Zero, Unlimited Bladeworks, and Heaven’s Feel) is ~50 episodes and 3 movies, so it’s similar length to AoT

Vtuber - Isekaijoucho – Sirius no Shinzou/The Heart of Sirius

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UKZt1vq8bKI 

Isekaijoucho, a VSinger from Kamitsubaki Studio, she debuted in February of 2020. Her voice is often described as calm and ethereal, and she has an incredibly consistent tone throughout her songs. During her early years, she was strongly considered as a "Virtual Dark Singer”, and although this is still true, she has since mastered her versatility as a singer, and has been shown to be able to show a wide range of emotions and performances, from complex VOCALOID songs such as “Heat Abnormal” to incredibly high energy iconic songs such as “Internet YAMERO” to emotional ballads such as her original song “Sirius no Shinzou” (Sirius's Heart) her most viewed song.

Composed and written by VOCALOID producer Kasamura Tota, the song itself was actually released less than a year after her debut, and currently sits as her most popular song ever. The song is an emotional ballad that explores themes of longing, grief, as well as an eternal connection. The name of the song is a metaphor based on the star Sirius A, which is most notably the brightest star that can be seen in our night sky, and namely it's a star that sits within a binary star system, and forever orbits its companion star, Sirius B. As such, the title serves as a metaphor for two souls that are inseparable, even when they appear far apart in the sky

[vtuber / video game] Ironmouse - FFXIV Fan Fest Stage Performance (3 Songs)
multiple links.
I saw these posted on the schedule a while back but since we haven't seen them yet, and since they're not in the current schedule, I wanted to request the lot to put them back on your radar. 

From what I gather the opportunity to perform at an event like this is quite rare; firstly because these performances are usually a paid event even at the expo, and secondly because hosting or licensing performances of their IP to tour operators is a significant line of business for Square Enix. The game is pretty special to her as well, she plays anonymously off stream to avoid extra attention and feel normal - and (if I recall correctly) because it helps her feel less isolated during holidays or during times when she's not able to stream. 

There are three songs in her stage performance, they were live streamed for free by the event (rare) and then released individually immediately afterward on her Youtube page. There's no link to the performance as a whole so you get three URLs for this, the song order in the performance was Give it All (https://youtu.be/Il2tevTVRpg), Sinister (https://youtu.be/W9aWPqYX60I), and Return to Oblivion (https://youtu.be/L7TzzBeV7vc). 

Edit: Per Bossjoe's suggestion - If you wanted to do a study, you could release the FFXIV part to youtube and do a couple of originals like Aren't We All the Worst (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nEgOSprI6A4) and Cry For Me (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OEHPmWMSudw) that were recently released from the Club Ironmouse concert. Cry For Me really broke containment a couple of months ago and has 45 million Spotify plays at this point.

Centimillimental - Fuyu no Hanashi (live)
[J-pop]
https://youtu.be/xgAbsJVtJLs?si=ZOYHPGXMFPAOMT70 

Centimillimental is a Japanese singer songwriter who became widely known after creating the music for the anime Given. Music plays a huge role in the series because the main characters are in bands, so he essentially created the entire musical identity of the two main bands, giving each of them their own unique sound. He's really an amazing musician and he does everything (vocals, piano, lyrics, composition, etc)

This is a really heartbreaking song created specifically for the show. The lyrics are about grieving someone you loved and for context, in the show this is the song the protagonist writes and sings to finally express the feelings and regrets he had been unable to put into words since their loved one died.

The song has two different versions because the music performed in the anime is actually sung by the voice actors themselves. So the anime band has its own version of the songs, while Centimillimental also released his own personal versions of several songs, and this is his version of Fuyu no Hanashi.

fifth time the charm ;)

Original post:

I am going to post this again:  it's a bit of a funny, different thing, though I suggest you take the two other songs as well, for something more serious. But I would love a reaction to Hololive's Omaru Polka' performance of her "Po" song at last year's 6th Holofes.

I am mostly intrigued by how, what (I think) is essentially a very simple song, is perceived by a professional as it is very fun and hype, but seemingly very very simple. I wonder what is something that a layperson like me would not realise about such aperformance? because while it might sound simply, it is probably not easy. 

Here is the  live video:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4C3kreEOk1Q&list=RD4C3kreEOk1Q&start_radio=1&t=4061s

Her performance starts around the 41.28 minute mark.

a little bit about Holofes, shamelessly stolen from the Hololive Fan Wiki:

"HoloFes concerts features nearly all Hololive members aside from ones that was then recently debuted. Songs selection contain a mix of cover songs as well as originals. Every featured members have their own solo segment as well as group songs. The event would take place at a real venue with a paid live stream as well a free live preview on the Hololive main channel on Youtube. 1st Fes was originally a single day event but the number of talents led to 2nd Fes onwards being organized as a multi-day event with the performing talents being splits between those days. Starting with 3rd Fes, Holofes was organized alongside HoloExpo. Each fes, Cover also make changes to the presentation, starting with AR integration in 2nd Fes, full virtual cameras for stream viewers in 3rd Fes, Guest artist Stage in 4th Fes and three screen setup at the venue for 5th Fes.

According to Yagoo, 1st Fes was the turning point for Hololive Production as a whole and helped secured the investment to expand its operation including the funding for its billion yen multimedia studio."

About this particular performance, Omaru Polka is from Hololive JP 5th generation, and it is part of the free experience; most of the concert is paid for either in person or livestream. Overall, it is just really a fun performance, and had the responsibility of hyping up the crowd on the second day, as Suisei did on the first day with Stellar Stellar. 

Probably most suited for a quick check rather than a very deep analysis. 


For a longer video, you could consider extending it to the next two songs and including Hakos Baelz, also known as "Bae" from Hololive English Promise (who you now have reacted to). Bae, as you have experienced, is known as an exceedingly great dancer. The third song would then be Houshou Marine from Hololive JP 3rd generation, who is the most subscribed active Hololive member, who performs in the Cube. 

Bae performs a cover of "Show" by Ado.
While Marine performs her original "How About Paipai Mask?"

Both of the two other songs are very different and more serious than the first one.

[Vocaloid] - Hitoshizuku×Yama△ - Blessed Messiah and the Tower of Ai

https://youtu.be/CiEC329xPos?si=nv4Xu6UZ46-BlsiA

One of my favorite Vocaloid songs of all time. This song is by the producer duo known as Hitoshizuku×Yama△ which consists of Hitoshizuku-P and Yamasankakkei. They're well known for their very theatrical storytelling songs utilizing usually multiple vocaloids with artwork for the MVs done by Suzunosuke. This song in particular uses Ten different vocaloids: (Hatsune Miku, Kagamine Rin & Len, KAITO, MEIKO, Megurine Luka, IA, Mayu, Gumi, and Gakupo). The instrumentals and arranging give a very dramatic and fantastical vibe and I think you'd love it!

This is the explanation of the songs story/meaning from the video's description:

 "Having searched for power which surpasses human intellect and mocked God, the civilization earned His anger. Once he sealed the 9 Components which make up the world in one tower, the world began its slow journey towards destruction.
It was then that a Messiah and their companions climbed the tower in order to beg God for forgiveness. After earning His forgiveness, they took the Blessed Light from the 9 Components and lit the world's lifespan at the altar. What followed was-the world's lifespan was connected by the light of the Messiah, who is created every 15 years.".

[J-POP] Akatsuki Records - Good Nightmare

https://youtu.be/UlV3VKnXDFM

(Apologies, this might be a long comment because it needs some context)
Because of your recent adventures into Vocaloid/vocal synths and utaites like Ado, I wanted to recommend something from what I would consider a sister community to Vocaloid- Touhou Project! Specifically, the singer named Stack from a fan music circle called Akatsuki Records.

It's a lot to summarize, but as best and as shortly as I can: Touhou Project is an indie video game series that grew in popularity alongside the rise of Vocaloid. There are many points of interest, but one of the most important is the music of Touhou. There are whole bands/music circles consisting of fans dedicated to just arranging/remixing music from the games (a lot of Vocaloid producers started/dipped their toes here too, such as Kikuo), and the one I am recommending is Akatsuki Records, who has a very unique vocalist named Stack. I've always wanted someone outside the scene to look at her work because her range and variety of sound is reminiscent of someone like Ado (to me, at least). The song I picked is not their most popular but is still well loved and I think a great introduction to her unique voice (if not maybe an odd one, haha). All vocals with in it are done by her.

If you cover this request and are interested in hearing more of her, I recommend the additional songs Third Eye Tango and Reverse Trigger. These songs I feel like introduce you to her range in sound pretty well.

4h

[JPOP, UTAITE] Wolpis Carter - Asu no Yozora Shoukaihan (Cover)

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Q91yh1VwxaU

Wolpis Carter's cover of "Asu no Yozora Shoukaihan" is basically the gold standard for male utaite high notes — he sings the whole thing in a bright, almost feminine range without sounding strained. It's the most viewed cover on Niconico for a reason: the raw emotion and control just hit differently, and once you hear it, you'll understand why people have been obsessed with it for a decade.

Wolpis re-uploaded his cover on Youtube exactly one decade after his Niconico video. It currently sits at 17.5 million views on the Japanese video platform and is the most viewed cover by any utaite ever on that website. 

Produced by Orangestar using the VOCALOID IA, "Asu no Yozora Shoukaihan" became a staple for covers because its soaring, bittersweet melody and wide dynamic range give singers like Wolpis Carter, Mafumafu etc. the perfect stage to show off their emotional control and high-note power

[K-pop/ Indie Rock] The Rose— Sorry

The Rose’s debut single Sorry was almost never released. Melody composed by drummer Hajoon and lyrics by singer Woosung. The Rose’s label at the time wanted them to do something more upbeat, and the label also wanted them to dance like typical K-pop idols. But The Rose never gave in and they managed to debut with Sorry, written by them alone. The song took off in Europe and the Americas to everyone’s surprise. Their first tour was a world tour around Europe. The Rose still to this day isn’t that popular in Korea because they are now an independent band that writes the music that they like and they don’t follow typical K-pop idol trends.

structured.name: Bennett
structured.text:
LGBTQ+ artists -
XLOV debuted last year with a “genderless” concept. All members have confirmed that they use all pronouns, and true to their concept, they do a crazy visual mix of masculine and feminine outfits and choreo.

structured.name: Bennett
structured.text:
They also make a point to do dance challenges (the easiest way K-pop artists ‘collab’) with idols who have heavily hinted that they are queer. And — as a gay guy myself, I think I can say this — their music is /gay/. Like at the very least, it is made FOR queer people.
They are technically categorized as a ‘boy group’, and admittedly are not *technically*, *explicitly* LGBTQ+ (because as mentioned before, that’s career suicide for K-pop). But I’m confident that if you were to poll the community

structured.text:
[K-POP] Taeyang - Eyes, nose, lips

https://youtu.be/UwuAPyOImoI?si=JuFIBAvJT0FQck5C

Taeyang just released a new album last month. Perhaps we can vote for a 5 song study along with this song?

This is Taeyang’s most famous solo song. It is about longing for a past lover. Taeyang wrote this song while on a break with his girlfriend (she is the girl in the photo in the MV). Perhaps the song worked because they got back together and are now married with a son.

structured.name: KJMx
structured.text:
[K-pop/ Indie Rock] The Rose— Sorry

The Rose’s debut single Sorry was almost never released. Melody composed by drummer Hajoon and lyrics by singer Woosung. The Rose’s label at the time wanted them to do something more upbeat, and the label also wanted them to dance like typical K-pop idols. But The Rose never gave in and they managed to debut with Sorry, written by them alone. The song took off in Europe and the Americas to everyone’s surprise. Their first tour was a world tour around Europe.

JUNE 2026: K-POP/Korean Artist Content Request & 🗓️Schedule
Tim Welch Vocal Studio

Yesterday

Edit

Happy June Everyone!

SO MUCH GRATITUDE 🙏

It's so much fun to get to know the world of K-pop and Korean artists through you all. You're such a warm and knowledgeable community, and I couldn't be more grateful 🥲. There are so many amazing artists from Korea to check out that I am honored to continue this study with you all long into the future. And know that it is only through your support that I keep this alive. If your favorite artist is not on the schedule yet, keep asking because I am listening to you all! And help each other out by connecting in the chats, to vote each other's ideas up.

💥Here's the SCHEDULE FOR THE MONTH! Expect tweaks!

📝 HOW TO REQUEST

One song or content idea per comment.

[K-POP] Artist — Song Title

Then drop the link and say whatever you want about it underneath!

💥If it's a cover — put whoever's SINGING it on the first line, and mention the original artist in your notes below.

GENRE LABEL:

If it's a song or musical piece, preferably add a genre identifier to help my crew organize the post for the CONTENT REQUEST TRACKER which is NOT FULLY UPDATED as of 6/1 btw. You could help me out by reporting any mistakes you see, there are definitely some.

And MOST IMPORTANTLY! 👇
💥VOTE ON OTHERS' POSTS WITH A ❤️ HEART...I COUNT THEM!

Thanks again everyone, I'm so excited for June. I'll see you in the chats!

Cheers,

Tim

LGBTQ+ artists -
XLOV debuted last year with a “genderless” concept. All members have confirmed that they use all pronouns, and true to their concept, they do a crazy visual mix of masculine and feminine outfits and choreo.

12
1
Bennett

·

2d

They also make a point to do dance challenges (the easiest way K-pop artists ‘collab’) with idols who have heavily hinted that they are queer. And — as a gay guy myself, I think I can say this — their music is /gay/. Like at the very least, it is made FOR queer people.
They are technically categorized as a ‘boy group’, and admittedly are not *technically*, *explicitly* LGBTQ+ (because as mentioned before, that’s career suicide for K-pop). But I’m confident that if you were to poll the community to ask if you should check out XLOV, especially during this month specifically, you’d get a big yes (if you want to give them a shot)

2d

[K-pop/ Indie Rock] The Rose— Sorry

The Rose’s debut single Sorry was almost never released. Melody composed by drummer Hajoon and lyrics by singer Woosung. The Rose’s label at the time wanted them to do something more upbeat, and the label also wanted them to dance like typical K-pop idols. But The Rose never gave in and they managed to debut with Sorry, written by them alone. The song took off in Europe and the Americas to everyone’s surprise. Their first tour was a world tour around Europe. The Rose still to this day isn’t that popular in Korea because they are now an independent band that writes the music that they like and they don’t follow typical K-pop idol trends.

Context:
Towa had a near career ending incident where a male voice was on her stream for a second and a large part of the JP fanbase left her overnight. One of her few english speaking JP fans made a Reddit post where the general EN fanbase saw and fully supported her. Thanks to the incident, within the JP branch she had the largest EN speaking fanbase by percentage until only a couple years ago due to gaining more JP fans. It was a vocaloid song but when Towa released the official cover, the pr

structured.name: waffling
structured.text:
Bury the Light - Casey Edwards

https://youtu.be/pvy9km7g6fw?si=-l67mNDh9CDgTLd3

This is a character battle theme from Devil May Cry V written by the same producer who did Ash Again by Gawr Gura. It is heavy, has great vocals by Victor Borba (slightly back in the mix since this plays during gameplay), and even though it's 9 minutes long it continues to introduce new musical ideas all the way through.

structured.name: waffling
structured.text: One side note: apparently the developers of Devil May Cry specifically asked Casey to not use any distorted guitar in this song, so instead he went and created the crunchiest, heaviest bass guitar sound he could imagine :D

structured.name: waffling
structured.text:
ERB's 3D Birthday Concert - Elizabeth Rose Bloodflame

https://www.youtube.com/live/PGppUhILuP4

This would be a longform reaction, the whole concert is about 50 minutes in length and shows off tons of sides of ERB's voice, as well as guest appearances from other Hololive members! It's full of covers of 80's Japanese Pop songs, it's really fun, has a bunch of great singing and it's a nice example of what 3D events can be like in the vtuber world

The MV is fun, the way it expresses feminity is real

I'm SUPER excited for May!

💥K-POP/KOREAN ARTISTS CONTENT REQUESTS 👉 HERE 👈

🚨IMPORTANT

Here are thet new rules for content requests moving forward so my crew can continue to organize the Heart Count Tracker better!

FOLLOW THIS MODEL👇

Artist — Song Title

YouTube link

(Optional: 1-2 sentences of context, requester's name, why it matters to you)

EXAMPLES

"FIRST TAKE — Ado

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=...

The Japanese live performance series — one mic, one take, no edits."

"Aaron Tveit — Last Night of the World (Miss Saigon)

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=...

Classic Broadway tenor moment — full belt with character clarity."

*Important to give content and links first, comments about it later in comment. Only one rec per comment is also SUPER important.

CONTENT SCHEDULE FOR MAY! 👇

https://www.timwelchmusic.com/tims-content-schedule

PAST CONTENT FROM APRIL YOU MAY HAVE MISSED!

It was such a huge month last month I'm just going to point you to this comprehensive COLLECTION to catch up!

Don't forget, your engagement in the community both on DISCORD and PATREON signals to me which content to prioritize! I'm so excited to see our community growing so much with so many awesome Ambassadors! Let's go! 🔥

Thank you!

Tim

Towa had a near career ending incident where a male voice was on her stream for a second and a large part of the JP fanbase left her overnight. Original post:

Fourth time the charm ;)

I am going to post this again:  it's a bit of a funny different thing, though I suggest you take the two other songs as well, for something more serious. But I would love a reaction to Hololive's Omaru Polka' performance of her "Po" song at last year's 6th Holofes.

I am mostly intrigued by how, what (I think) is essentially a very simple song, is perceived by a professional as it is very fun and hype, but seemingly very very simple. I wonder what is something that a layperson like me would not realise about such aperformance? because while it might sound simply, it is probably not easy. 

Here is the  live video:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4C3kreEOk1Q&list=RD4C3kreEOk1Q&start_radio=1&t=4061s

Her performance starts around the 41.28 minute mark.

a little bit about Holofes, shamelessly stolen from the Hololive Fan Wiki:

"HoloFes concerts features nearly all Hololive members aside from ones that was then recently debuted. Songs selection contain a mix of cover songs as well as originals. Every featured members have their own solo segment as well as group songs. The event would take place at a real venue with a paid live stream as well a free live preview on the Hololive main channel on Youtube. 1st Fes was originally a single day event but the number of talents led to 2nd Fes onwards being organized as a multi-day event with the performing talents being splits between those days. Starting with 3rd Fes, Holofes was organized alongside HoloExpo. Each fes, Cover also make changes to the presentation, starting with AR integration in 2nd Fes, full virtual cameras for stream viewers in 3rd Fes, Guest artist Stage in 4th Fes and three screen setup at the venue for 5th Fes.

According to Yagoo, 1st Fes was the turning point for Hololive Production as a whole and helped secured the investment to expand its operation including the funding for its billion yen multimedia studio."

About this particular performance, Omaru Polka is from Hololive JP 5th generation, and it is part of the free experience; most of the concert is paid for either in person or livestream. Overall, it is just really a fun performance, and had the responsibility of hyping up the crowd on the second day, as Suisei did on the first day with Stellar Stellar. 

Probably most suited for a quick check rather than a very deep analysis. 


For a longer video, you could consider extending it to the next two songs and including Hakos Baelz, also known as "Bae" from Hololive English Promise (who you now have reacted to). Bae, as you have experienced, is known as an exceedingly great dancer. The third song would then be Houshou Marine from Hololive JP 3rd generation, who is the most subscribed active Hololive member, who performs in the Cube. 

Bae performs a cover of "Show" by Ado.
While Marine performs her original "How About Paipai Mask?"

Both of the two other songs are very different and more serious than the first one.

Jerry Leung

3w
Show more

Ayunda Risu - MIR//or

https://youtu.be/QIIbdZOBfyM

Since we have checked out ALiCE&u, thought this might be a  good one to check out too! In the MV version of ALiCE&u she switched to the "Risu" voice which is less noticable, but MIR//or is a full on duet of "Ayunda" and "Risu". A common interpretation of the song is that "Risu" represents her Vtuber self, who has gain a level of successfulness and was even a bit full of herself, while her true self "Ayunda" feels alienated from the love she is receiving because those love is for her virtual self. The song is a process of her struggling with these conflicting emotions and working towards accepting that both Ayunda and Risu are just different sides of her and they were able to make peace and coexist. I personally like this song a lot because not only it feels like a spiritual successor to ALiCE&u, it's also more complicated in terms of composition, a more challenging song vocally, and expresses a more complex feeling that takes a while to sink in. The song is mostly in English and Indonesian but there are official subtitles that comes with the YouTube video. Hope you'll like this!

Ayreon is the music project of Dutch musician/song writer/producer Arjen Lucassen. He does most of the songwriting and plays many instruments on his tracks but is famous for bringing in other musicians and singers for all of his projects. This album features 11 different singers, all of which are featured in this song, who all play a role within the story that is told through album.

Most of them are singers from rock and metal bands in Europe and I will gladly provide a list of the personnel but I'd rather wait until after you do reaction, if this makes it to that point.

What's great about this is he adds a lot of behind the scenes commentary about this album, the singers, and the story. 

There's a wide range of voices between all the singers that will give you a lot to break down and talk about. Hopefully this is something people will vote for and you can get to it some point.

Show more

Centimillimental - Fuyu no Hanashi (live)
https://youtu.be/xgAbsJVtJLs?si=ZOYHPGXMFPAOMT70 

Centimillimental is a Japanese singer songwriter who became widely known after creating the music for the anime Given. Music plays a huge role in the series because the main characters are in bands, so he essentially created the entire musical identity of the two main bands, giving each of them their own unique sound. He's really an amazing musician and he does everything (vocals, piano, lyrics, composition, etc)

This is a really heartbreaking song created specifically for the show. The lyrics are about grieving someone you loved and for context, in the show this is the song the protagonist writes and sings to finally express the feelings and regrets he had been unable to put into words since their loved one died.

The song has two different versions because the music performed in the anime is actually sung by the voice actors themselves. So the anime band has its own version of the songs, while Centimillimental also released his own personal versions of several songs, and this is his version of Fuyu no Hanashi.

[Video Game] Final Fantasy XIV - Tomorrow and Tomorrow (Amanda Achen and the Eorzean Symphony)

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5fgB3M2y4wY

I figured your channel could use some Final Fantasy XIV requests :) FFXIV is a MMORPG that has been going for well over a decade and has some of the richest, most varied music I have heard in a MMORPG. It's extremely story-driven, making the music especially important, and as such, has quite a few tear jerkers that pull you into this storyline, and this stuff sticks with you despite the passing years. This song from the Shadowbringers expansion is one of those songs. It was a popular leitmotif in the expansion's music and truly flourished on its own at the end of it. Amanda Achen's voice together with Masayoshi Soken's masterful songwriting take us on a journey every damn time. I'm hopeful you might enjoy a lot more songs from the game as time goes. :)

4

Kumo Desu Ga, Nani ka Ending Song Full 『Ganbare! Kumoko-San』 by Aoi Yuuki

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lZqbgn3SvmU

Kumo Desu ga, Nani ka? (also known as So I'm a Spider, So What?) is an isekai story following an entire Japanese high school class reincarnated into a fantasy RPG world. Our MC is reincarnated as a spider while her classmates are heroes of the world. Ganbare! Kumoko-San means "Good luck, Kumoko-San (kumo = spider, ko from the kanji kodomo which means child, she refers herself as spider child because she has no name when reincarnated and was born after being hatched from a spider egg). Genuinely a fun song to listen because it's weird. Just wanted to share something that I thought was pretty fun to listen to and appreciate the work and creativity that went into it. The song is about being frustrated that she is a spider and struggling to survive, the grind of an Isekai RPG game (most of the song i think). Aoi Yuuki is actually a voice actor for a lot of roles in anime. Those that have watched anime for some time are able to recognise her because she has quite a unique voice and speaks so fast that she needs to be asked to slow down lol.

structured.name: Dani
structured.text:
aespa - supernova
https://youtu.be/phuiiNCxRMg?list=RDphuiiNCxRMg

Supernova is one of aespa's weirder songs. One of the producers is Dem Jointz, who is known for his experimental songs (and the famous producer tag "incoming"). Supernova and Armageddon were the double-title tracks of their album, and the aespa girls themselves bet that Armageddon would be more successful, but Supernova had PAK (number 1 on all Korean music charts) and won song of the year at many K-pop awards shows.

K-POP/Korean Artist Content Requests MAY 2026
Tim Welch Vocal Studio

May 1

Edit

VERY EXCITED FOR MAY!

New rules for content requests moving forward so my crew can to organize the heart count tracker. We are still honoring the corrections you all have suggested from past months, stay tuned for that! Also, if I haven't gotten to it yet and it's on the tracker, request it again! Hearts accumulate.

💥I will still take new requests and react to them this month even though the schedule is done!

FOLLOW THIS MODEL FOR COMMENTS ON THIS POST👇

Artist — Song Title

YouTube link

(Optional: 1-2 sentences of context, requester's name, why it matters to you)

EXAMPLES

"KILLING VOICE— Day6

https://www.youtube.com/wa...

They are AMAZING! I love their song...."

"Hyunjin (Stray Kids) — LOVER

https://www.youtube.com/w...

This song kills me! His self-produced solo, emotional context with ...."

*Important to give content and links first, comments about it later in comment. Only one rec per comment is also SUPER important.

CONTENT SCHEDULE FOR MAY! 👇

https://www.timwelchmusic.com/tims-content-schedule

PAST CONTENT FROM APRIL YOU MAY HAVE MISSED!

It was such a huge month I'm just going to point you to the K-POP COLLECTION to catch up!

Don't forget, your engagement in the community both on DISCORD and PATREON signals to me which content to prioritize! I'm SO EXCITED to see the community growing so much! I'm so excited about this month, be sure to keep an eye on the schedule!

Cheers,

Tim

ATEEZ — Coachella full performance (preferably Weekend 1 when tensions were highest)

Weekend 1 on x (it's not on youtube in its entirety anywhere): https://x.com/1024brainrot/status/1904610702809068025
(or if you can only do on yt, here's a Weekend 2 fancam which doesn't have as good audio as the weekend 1 on x does: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_SJn072gHHE& )

ATEEZ had the huge honor and pressure of being the first kpop boy group ever to play Coachella, invited when other "higher status" boy groups from the Big4 agencies had not been because of their spectacular LIVE singing and performance skills. They went all-out doing live rock band rearrangements of all their songs for their Coachella set, took some time to showcase some Korean culture during one song ("The Real"), and even brought their pirate roots to life during their epic closer -- the orchestral version of WONDERLAND from KINGDOM (yes, *including* Jongho's B5 live at the *end* of their 50-minute set!). Especially during Weekend 1 when there was so much dry desert wind blowing that you can visually see the dust clouds (and it made them look even more epic and exciting!), what they manage to do with their live singing in this insanely high-stakes, intense first weekend performance was *insane,* absolutely lightning in a bottle, and I'd love to hear your thoughts and deeper vocal analysis of what they did!

Supernova and Armageddon were the double-title tracks of their album, and the aespa girls themselves bet that Armageddon would be more successful, but Supernova had PAK (number 1 on all Korean music charts) and won song of the year at many K-pop awards shows.

K-POP/KOREAN ARTIST SONG REQUEST POST — APRIL 2026
Tim Welch Vocal Studio

April 1

Edit

Hey K-Pop Crew!

March was MASSIVE.

A warm welcome 🤝 to all the new ARMY who came over after the BTS ARIRANG album reaction — WELCOME! To all who just watched us start Kingdom: Legendary War — THIS IS GOING TO BE FUN.

K-POP/KOREAN ARTIST requests for APRIL below as comments👇 SEE CHANGES BELOW!

💥NEW: Kingdom Thursday is HERE! We're watching Kingdom: Legendary War together every Thursday — $10 tier. ATEEZ, BTOB, SF9, Stray Kids, iKON, THE BOYZ. If you want me to deep dive any specific performance, drop it below!

💥A SMALL CHANGE TO MONTHLY SONG REQUESTS-(paying members only)

Some of you are SO passionate about your favorite artists that we've had people creating extra free accounts to boost heart counts. I'm flattered that there is so much passion in the community for favorites artists to be seen, but it unfortunately messes with the data and it's not fair to others.

So starting this month, song request posts are for paying members. Every heart = a real person. Your votes carry even more weight now!

Nobody's in trouble. You're just enthusiastic and I love it.

💥Comment your song ideas below on THIS POST BELOW which is EXCLUSIVE to PAYING MEMBERS. 👇

ALL PAST SONG REQUESTS IN THIS 👉 LINK!

Check this link on my website to see all heart counts on song requests from the last few months. *Just use cmd+F or ctrl+F to search for your song or artist on the page.

YOUR BIGGEST QUESTION

"Should I STILL add my song request again this month if it's on that list?"

YES, DO THAT! If you get hearts on your post for this month, I add them onto the hearts from other months, they are cumulative. It will push your song EVEN more to the front of the pack.

This 👉 page will only be updated AT THE BEGINNING OF EVERY MONTH, all song requests for this month should go on this post below as a comment like usual. 👇

And don't worry, I'm aware of which song requests had fake heart counts on them, thanks for the help in finding them! 🙏

🔥 MARCH — DELIVERED

If you requested it, THANK YOU — your hearts made it happen:

✅ BTS "ARIRANG" Full Album (90 min!) ✅ BTS "Swim" Song & Vocal Analysis ✅ BTS Trailer & "ARIRANG" Reaction ✅ Kingdom: Legendary War Ep 1 — NEW SHOW! ✅ Stray Kids "Bounce Back" — Binnie! ✅ Stray Kids "Endless Sun" + "Side Effects" Deep Dive ✅ Stray Kids "Karma" Full Album ✅ Taemin "Heaven" with Custom Intro — Ashley! ✅ NMIXX "Party O'Clock" ✅ SB19 x Jolin "Emoji" — on YouTube! ✅ Red Velvet "Feel My Rhythm" + "Cosmic" ✅ ENHYPEN Jay x Yuuri "Dry Flowers" ✅ BLACKPINK "Deadline" Full EP ✅ Forestella "Despacito" + "Bohemian Rhapsody"

📝 HOW TO REQUEST

BE SUPER RESPECTFUL OF EACH OTHER 🙏 This is all about celebrating artists.

❤️ HEART EACH OTHERS' COMMENTS: This is everything! PRO TIP: Find your fellow fans on Discord and coordinate — Patreon doesn't let you message each other directly.

SHOUTOUTS! I'll shout you out if I pick your comment.

HOW LONG? (optional) QUICK TAKE (5-7 min) — $5 SONG ANALYSIS (15-30 min) — $5 DEEP DIVE (30+ min) — $10

🎤 K-POP I'VE COVERED SO FAR 👉 Full list in the K-POP COMPLETE COLLECTION

STRAY KIDS — KV Deep Analysis, Lollapalooza (3 parts), Karma Full Album 💥, Bounce Back 💥, Endless Sun 💥, Side Effects Deep Dive 💥, Recording Scene MEGAVERSE, Thunderous, Megaverse, Venom, Escape, Seungmin BoaF, CASE 143, I.N Hallucination, Hollow, God's Menu, MANIAC, Red Lights, Christmas EveL, Chk Chk Boom, CINEMA, Divine, Ceremony, Do It, Felix Deep End, Bang Chan Railway, JJAM

ATEEZ — KV Deep Analysis (1:09), Beginner's Guide, Cyberpunk 40-min Deep Dive, Mingi ROAR, Lemon Drop + ARRIBA + DJANGO, Guerrilla, Love Poem

KINGDOM: LEGENDARY WAR 💥 — Episode 1 (ATEEZ, BTOB, SF9)

BTS — ARIRANG Full Album (90 min) 💥, Swim 💥, ARIRANG Trailer 💥, Backstory, Tiny Desk (56 min), Magic Shop, Agust D, ON, Dynamite, Fake Love (40 min), Idol, Not Today, i need u, Dope, Black Swan

ENHYPEN — KV Full Analysis (1:09), Backstory, Fatal Trouble, Jay Bon Jovi Cover, No Doubt, Shout Out Olympic Song

RED VELVET — KV Deep Dive (84 min!), Backstory, Psycho, Cosmic 💥, Feel My Rhythm Deep Dive 💥

EXO / BAEKHYUN — EXO KV Deep Analysis, Obsession, Ringa Ringa Ring Live, Cry For Love, Bambi, Lemonade, Rendez-Vous

SHINEE / TAEMIN — SHINee 1st Listen, Taemin Danger, MOVE, Heaven with Custom Intro 💥, Jonghyun Let Me Out, Key Hunter

NMIXX — Backstory + Tiny Desk, High Horse, SPINNIN' ON IT, Blue Valentine, Party O'Clock 💥

FORESTELLA 💥 — Despacito, Champions, Bohemian Rhapsody Deep Analysis

BLACKPINK — Deadline Full EP 💥, Lisa, Rosé, Jennie, Jisoo solos

DREAMCATCHER — JUSTICE Band Live, Vision

OTHER — XG THE CORE Full Album, KATSEYE Lollapalooza, WOODZ Drowning + I'll Never Love Again, MONSTA X KV, IU KV + Love Poem, TXT Runaway + NO LABELS, LE SSERAFIM Perfect Night, IVE I Am, BTOB I'll Be Your Man, Wings DOPAMINE

K-POP DEMON HUNTERS — Full movie + all soundtrack breakdowns + covers

💬 FIND YOUR FELLOW FANS K-POP CHAT on Patreon and Discord! Coordinate which songs to heart!

Thank you for being here. Your support keeps K-Pop in heavy rotation both here AND on YouTube.

Let's make April amazing. Drop requests below! 👇

Tim 🙌🔥

Stray Kids DominATE Experience Movie

I put this in last month but I think it got a little mangled because I ramble so much, so here's a shorter post with the name at the top. This would be a BIG reaction, the movie is ~2.5 hours long. It is currently the ONLY recording of an entire Stray Kids concert that you can stream (all the rest are virtual live or blu-ray only). 

It's the DominATE concert at SoFi stadium in LA, California. They've added in some interviews with the members, and also I believe they have clipped out some of the crowd interaction segments, for the sake of time. VERY good and shows how they really are an international phenomenon with this insane turnout in California (sold out SoFi 2 days in a row. The stadium can hold like 70k-100k people).

https://www.amazon.com/gp/video/detail/B0GJMW82YB/ref=atv_dp_share_cu_r

Show more

Stray Kids' Hyunjin LOVER
Hyunjin released this solo song on his birthday out of the blue, and it's so good. It has a very different style from a lot of their other music, was written/composed by Hyunjin (along with some credited collaborators you can see in the song description). The video is lovely and artsy and sad. I really hope we get more of this emo/alt rock Hyunjin.

A little piece of info about why this song is so special: last year, Hyunjin announced in a very emotional comment at the DominATE: CelebrATE concert that he had problems with his voice (to the point where he couldn't even speak sometimes) and was afraid he might have to leave the group. Fortunately he worked with a vocal coach and was able to rehabilitate his voice. This song with its unique vocal style and emotional power I think shows how he was able to come back stronger as a vocalist. And all this is from one of the main DANCERS; he has always worked so hard to become a well-rounded artist in every aspect. I am so proud of him. 

https://youtu.be/l8sSQAa1kNM?si=-NCDg59_YnlJtXh5

Day6 Killing Voice - repost from March

id loooove to see you do a deep dive with DAY6 Killing Voice!
they are a band under JYPE (same company as Stray Kids and NMIXX)
They've got 3 main powerhouse vocalists, each so uniquely tonally different from each other 
LORE!:
They're the first band to come from JYPE (paraphrasing here but) it's been said that the band was created because the members weren't the most skilled in dance in the sense to be in a traditional kpop group, but JYPE didn't want to miss out on debuting such skilled and talented voices so they put them in a band together instead.
Since their beginning they've created and written all their own music, and sort of had a grand underdog story, where only in their like 8th year after debut they blew up and like 3 of their songs including their debut song topped the charts domestically in south korea for a long while

A new request, a girl group which has been recommended before I think: "i-dle." They might be somewhat controversial since they had to let go of a member, Soojin, in 2021. But I still support Soyeon and her leadership of the group. They did a Killing Voice, so that can be a nice introduction if you are interested. (The Killing Voice was done after Soojin's departure). It is linked below:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=et3VDbL7cIA

I really appreciate Yuqi's voice (in the KV video, she is the idol with the blonde hair). She's another example of an atypical "K-POP" voice, naturally lower. 

Oh, I almost forgot! This year, or maybe it was 2025, the group dropped the (g) from their group name - they used to be called (g)i-dle (said like, "gee idol"). In case you saw their old name, and was confused.

structured.text:
Vtuber- Michi Mochieevee- Medicine

structured.name: Midnyte64
structured.text:
And when you consider that she is on regular medication, and has some ongoing health issues. The fact that she was in the fashion industry, and so many other parts about her, it really feels to me like Medicine can relate to many parts of her life.

This album features 11 different singers, all of which are featured in this song, who all play a role within the story that is told through album.

I'll also point you at someone new.

Meet Courtney Hadwin.

She came to fame in 2018 on America's Got Talent at the age of 14. She is a prodigy, and she has a voice like Joplin's and channeled the sound of R&B, Soul, and old fashioned Rock and Roll in an uncanny way. She immediatly became The. Next. Big. Thing. She didn't win her swason though, but got enough lift that she was on the touring circuit, doing red carpets, and interviews heavily.

structured.name: Jevon Kasitch
structured.text:
For the start of the April review requests I'll add in the next Chappell piece that I think deserves some attention. 

Sugar High

This song was released by Chappell under her Atlantic Contract in 2018. It was the last piece added to the School Nights EP that Atlantic released to bridge the slow development pace they were experiencing with Chappell on her first album. 

Sugar High holds an interesting place in Chappell's discography and development. It's the last thing she would do for Atlantic

🔥62 POSTS IN MARCH! April Song Requests
Tim Welch Vocal Studio

April 1

Edit

Hey everyone!

March was AMAZING, thank you SO MUCH for the song recs and for jumping into the community chats more!

💥PATREON THEMED DAYS-(in addition to normal weekly posting)

Engagement has been great so far on all of the days of the week so I'll be keeping the schedule as is for now. If the community shifts, I may sub a day for something else moving forward (themed days content mostly lives at the $10 tier since most are long form over 30 minutes.)

Monday: 🎭 Musical Theatre Monday — Death Note Musical is our first!

Tuesday: ⚔️ Attack on Tuesday — AoT Season 2

Wednesday: 🤖 VTuber Wednesday — rotating picks (We will DEEP DIVE Vtubers)

Thursday: 👑 Kingdom Thursday — Kingdom: Legendary War

Friday: 📓 Anime Friday — Death Note

Saturday: 😈 Sinful Saturday — The Amazing Digital Circus (NEW!)

Sunday: 🇪🇺 Eurovision Sunday — through Grand Final May 16

💥K-POP APRIL 2026👉 SONG REQUEST POST 👈

Song requests for April below as comments👇 — but first, let me catch you up.

💥A SMALL CHANGE TO MONTHLY SONG REQUESTS-(paying members only)

Some of you are SO passionate about your favorite artists that we've had people creating extra free accounts to boost heart counts. I'm flattered that there is so much passion in the community for favorites artists to be seen, but it unfortunately messes with the data and it's not fair to others.

So starting this month, song request posts are for paying members. Every heart = a real person. Your votes carry even more weight now!

Nobody's in trouble. You're just enthusiastic and I love it.

💥Comment your song ideas below on THIS POST BELOW which is EXCLUSIVE to PAYING MEMBERS. 👇

ALL PAST SONG REQUESTS IN THIS 👉 LINK!

Check this link on my website to see all heart counts on song requests from the last few months. *Just use cmd+F or ctrl+F to search for your song or artist on the page.

YOUR BIGGEST QUESTION

"Should I STILL add my song request again this month if it's on that list?"

YES, DO THAT! If you get hearts on your post for this month, I add them onto the hearts from other months, they are cumulative. It will push your song EVEN more to the front of the pack.

This 👉 page will only be updated AT THE BEGINNING OF EVERY MONTH, all song requests for this month should go on this post below as a comment like usual. 👇

And don't worry, I'm aware of which song requests had fake heart counts on them, thanks for the help in finding them! 🙏

💥MARCH — DELIVERED

Here's what got DONE. If you requested it, THANK YOU.

K-POP 🎵 BTS 'ARIRANG' Full Album (90 min) — $10 🎵 Kingdom: Legendary War Ep 1 — NEW THURSDAY SHOW! $10 🎵 Stray Kids 'Bounce Back' 🎵 NMIXX 'Party O'Clock' — WalkLikeAHuman! 🎵 Taemin 'Heaven' with Custom Intro — Ashley! 🎵 Red Velvet 'Feel My Rhythm' Deep Dive 🎵 ENHYPEN Jay x Yuuri 'Dry Flowers' 🎵 BLACKPINK 'Deadline' Full EP 🎵 Stray Kids 'Karma' Full Album 🎵 SB19 x Jolin 'Emoji' — on YouTube!

FORESTELLA — NEW ARTIST! 36 new members from 3 posts. YOU spoke. 🎵 Forestella 'Champions' First Listen 🎵 'Despacito' — on YouTube! 🎵 'Bohemian Rhapsody' Deep Analysis — $10

HAZBIN HOTEL — SEASON 2 COMPLETE! 🎵 S2 Episodes 5-8 including FINALE — $10 🎵 'Hear My Hope' Song Analysis — on YouTube!

ANIME 🎵 Death Note Episode 1 — NEW FRIDAY SHOW! $10 🎵 Attack on Titan S2 — continuing every Tuesday! $10

MUSICAL THEATRE 🎵 Death Note Musical Part 1 — NEW MONDAY SHOW! $10

VTUBER 🎵 Ayunda Risu 'Alice&U' Deep Dive — $10 🎵 Hololive Countdown Live — $10 🎵 Chinatown Blues — Neuro & Vedal 🎵 Ironmouse 'Stay Focused' — on YouTube!

ROCK / METAL 🎵 BMTH 'Shadow Moses' RAH — Jacob Thomas! 🎵 Leprous 'The Price' — Queen Mistletoe! 🎵 Joanne Shaw Taylor — Gaye McCullough! 🎵 The Warning 'Kerosene' 🎵 Maphra 'Doomed' + Comparison — on YouTube!

EUROVISION 🎵 Serbia LAVINA 'Kraj Mene' — on YouTube! (7,000+ views/day) 🎵 Serbia Deep Dive — $10 🎵 Australia 'Scream' — on YouTube!

RAYE 🎵 Full Album 'This Music May Contain Hope' — $10 🎵 'Nightingale Lane' 🎵 'Click Clack Symphony' — on YouTube!

J-POP 🎵 Eve 'Dramaturgy' — _firey, DELIVERED! 🎵 Godish — Miku & Ado Deep Study 🎵 Ado 'AiAiA' First Take — on YouTube!

💥I SEE YOU

🔜 SEVENTEEN Killing Voice — Bennett, Elodie, hannah jade. In the queue. 🔜 BTOB Killing Voice —after Seventeen! 🔜 Forestella — Zaphne & Maria, your patience PAID OFF. More coming. 🔜 The Rose "Back to Me" — blkrose_22, Elsa. Tracked. 🔜 Ado "Fleeting Lullaby" + Phantom Siita — Dorsann, Cricket 🔜 Oshi No Ko "Idol" — PlayChris310 🔜 iKON — Zoey, 18 hearts! 🔜 Chanmina "Test Me" — Sharon 🔜 Calliope "End of a Life" — Bossjoe 🔜 Minami "Crying for Rain" — Dorsann 🔜 Yorushika — Dorsann + Alliterative Alias

💥COMING IN APRIL

🎵 More Forestella — you brought lots of new members. I see you. 🎵 The Amazing Digital Circus starts Saturday, with Hazbin and Helluva content continuing to drop through the week! 🎵 Eurovision every Sunday through May 16 and quick takes through the week 🎵 All five weekly shows continue 🎵 More VTuber, more K-Pop, more everything!

💥HOW TO REQUEST

Post NON-K-POP requests as comments below. K-POP goes on the 👉K-POP APRIL POST👈.

LINKS: YouTube link in your comment. HEARTS: Heart each other's comments — that's how I know what to prioritize! SHOUTOUTS: I'll shout you out if I pick your request.

💥FIND YOUR PEOPLE

Patreon Chats: K-POP | J-POP & ANIME | P-POP & SEA | EUROPE & TURKEY | VTUBER & VGM | MUSICAL THEATRE | METAL & ROCK Discord

💥FOR FREE MEMBERS

$5/month: 3x more content, song analyses, blocked reactions, request votes, chat access.

$5 SONG COLLECTION (465 songs and growing!)

$10/month: Everything above PLUS weekly shows, Killing Voices, full concerts, 90+ hours of long-form content.

$10 POST COLLECTION (159 posts and climbing!)

Thank you for being here. Thank you for trusting me with your favorite artists and for supporting the channel!

It is only because of all of you that I am able to keep this going.

See you in the chats!

Tim

Posted April 1 at 2:01 PM

2mo

And when you consider that she is on regular medication, and has some ongoing health issues. The fact that she was in the fashion industry, and so many other parts about her, it really feels to me like Medicine can relate to many parts of her life. 

Hell, even the fact from what I heard that she almost took her own life while in Nijisanji...and then you consider that her cover of Medicine is the first song I recall coming from her, after leaving Nijisanji, and her claiming she couldn't sing all the way up to it's release and afterward even...it is so emotional to me, and hits me so hard.

From last month (third times the charm :D):

I am going to post this again,  it's a bit of a funny different thing ( though I suggest you take the two other songs aswell for something more serious). But I would love a reaction to Hololive's Omaru Polka' performance of her "Po" song at last year's 6th Holofes.

I am mostly intrigued by how, what (I think) is essentially a very simple song, is perceived by a professional as it is very fun and hype. But what is something that a layperson like me would not realise about the performance?

Here is the  live video:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4C3kreEOk1Q&list=RD4C3kreEOk1Q&start_radio=1&t=4061s

Her performance starts around the 41.28 minute mark.

a little bit about Holofes, shamelessly stolen from the Hololive Fan Wiki:

"HoloFes concerts features nearly all Hololive members aside from ones that was then recently debuted. Songs selection contain a mixed of cover songs as well as originals. Every featured members have their own solo segment as well as group songs. The event would take place at a real venue with a paid live stream as well a free live preview on the Hololive main channel on Youtube. 1st Fes was originally a single day event but the number of talents led to 2nd Fes onwards being organized as a multi-day event with the performing talents being splits between those days. Starting with 3rd Fes, Holofes was organized alongside HoloExpo. Each fes, Cover also make changes to the presentation, starting with AR integration in 2nd Fes, full virtual cameras for stream viewers in 3rd Fes, Guest artist Stage in 4th Fes and three screen setup at the venue for 5th Fes.

According to Yagoo, 1st Fes was the turning point for Hololive Production as a whole and helped secured the investment to expand its operation including the funding for its billion yen multimedia studio."

About this particular performance, Omaru Polka is from Hololive JP 5th generation, and it is part of the free experience; most of the concert is paid for either in person or livestream. Overall, it is just really a fun performance, and had the responsibility of hyping up the crowd on the second day, as Suisei did on the first day with Stellar Stellar. 

Hololive 7th Fes is happening 6-8th of march :) 

Probably most suited for a quick check rather than a very deep analysis. 


For a longer video, you could consider extending it to the next two songs, as well as including Hakos Baelz, also known as "Bae" from Hololive English Promise. Who is known as an exceedingly great dancer. The third song would then be Houshou Marine from Hololive JP 3rd generation, who is the most subscribed active Hololive member, who performs in the Cube. 

Both of the two other songs are very different and more serious than the first one.

Ayreon - "The Day That the World Breaks Down"
https://youtu.be/oFuMKdrzPqU?si=JoEA5LRMXRK9vh3J

Ayreon is the music project of Dutch musician/song writer/producer Arjen Lucassen. He does most of the songwriting and plays many instruments on his tracks but is famous for bringing in other musicians and singers for all of his projects. This album features 11 different singers, all of which are featured in this song, who all play a role within the story that is told through album.

Most of them are singers from rock and metal bands in Europe and I will gladly provide a list of the personnel but I'd rather wait until after you do reaction, if this makes it to that point.

What's great about this is he adds a lot of behind the scenes commentary about this album, the singers, and the story. 

There's a wide range of voices between all the singers that will give you a lot to break down and talk about. Hopefully this is something people will vote for and you can get to it some point.

Probably the most emotional duet song from hololive, with a ton of history behind it. As you learned in "The Abandoned VTuber that Made It", AZKi and Suisei were briefly in the music-focused INoNaKa Music branch together. While Suisei was neglected in the branch until she was transfered to the main hololive branch, AZKi was properly supported; however, the plan with INNK was that it would only run for 3 years (2019-2022), after which AZKi would graduate. AZKi created this song for Suisei's first in person concert towards the end of those 3 years as a farewell celebrating her time with Suisei and wishing Suisei to ascend to heights she hadn't; however, thanks to the encouragement from Suisei and other members, when AZKi's time in INNK came to an end she transferred to the main branch instead of graduating. At the 2024 hololive festival Capture the Moment, the pair sang this special version of the song, with the roles of Suisei and AZKi in the song's final chorus reversed and the lyrics changed by Suisei to reflect that they were now continuing into the future together. Any dry eyes remaining in the audience were promptly flooded right after the performance when Suisei read a letter to AZKi recounting their journey and thanking her for sticking around. They performed this version of the song again at AZKi's in person concert last year, with this time AZKi being the one to make everyone cry afterward as she read her own letter to Suisei in reply to Suisei's from the year prior.

I'll also point you at someone new.

Meet Courtney Hadwin.

She came to fame in 2018 on America's Got Talent at the age of 14. She is a prodigy, and she has a voice like Joplin's and channeled the sound of R&B, Soul, and old fashioned Rock and Roll in an uncanny way. She immediatly became The. Next. Big. Thing. She didn't win her swason though, but got enough lift that she was on the touring circuit, doing red carpets, and interviews heavily. 

Sadly it was all a bit much, and she declined into a low key emotional breakdown by 2020, and ended up withdrawing from the industry and performing for a while to look after her own health and well being. 

Over the last year she has reappeared. Playing small clubs and venues to the delight of her core fanbase which has stayed loyal to her through the years. She seems to have recentered herself and is starting to persue things again at her pace and in her own way. 

Here she is performing "Pretty Little Thing" in Birmingham 4 days ago. It was coupled with the announcement that she'll be touring again this summer.  The song is an original by her by the way. 

Tim, this girl is worth reviewing. She's survived, and rebuilt herself and I think she has a very solid future ahead, and it's only a matter of time until the world catches up. 

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7bsMehV-xPo

For the start of the April review requests I'll add in the next Chappell piece that I think deserves some attention. 

Sugar High

This song was released by Chappell under her Atlantic Contract in 2018. It was the last piece added to the School Nights EP that Atlantic released to bridge the slow development pace they were experiencing with Chappell on her first album. 

Sugar High holds an interesting place in Chappell's discography and development. It's the last thing she would do for Atlantic before Pink Pony Club, and her change of focus and direction two years later. But in it we can hear that she was already shifting off the style of the other School Nights pieces in some subtle ways that would become stylistic highlights of her sound Pink Pony Club onwards. There is a lot more of her signature vocal flips and runs. 

The video also is her first 'abstract one' that contails a lot of subtle little things that point to the changes in her personal life that she was starting to experience in this period. 

It's still in the School Nights Cursive style, but it's not as strong. And the Instramentation also shows a widening of influances and styles that she'd fully embrace in the future once she'd hook up with Dan.

. 
I think this would be a good review candidate for you to take up Tim, with the added bonus that you could place it on YouTube with NO copyright issues. Atlantic has long since given up on protecting the School Nights material they still control which Sugar High is one of. Chappell has not performed it live in most of a decade, and while she has reprised other School Nights works on rare occasions, Sugar High ha never been touched again. 

Also there are NO major reviews by any reactor up for Sugar High. So this can keep you connected to the Chappell fanbase as we wait for new material. 

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=O4RARnYN7Sw

structured.name: Dani
structured.text:
Red Velvet Feel-My Rhythm - Deep Dive. This song samples Bach's "Air on G String.", music video has many references to famous paintings, especially H. Bosch's "The Garden of Earthly Delights," and tells the story of the transition from winter to spring (the music video was released on the March equinox, which is also Bach's birthday).
The perfect song to showcase their combined Red and Velvet concept.
MV https://youtu.be/R9At2ICm4LQ?list=RDR9At2ICm4LQ 
Recording Behind https://youtu.be/ekAlRHP4F

structured.name: Nicole
structured.text:
PLAVE Killing Voice
https://youtu.be/LDemgZcBj7o?si=NSWn5yTrx7lZb__u

PLAVE is a virtual k-pop group comprised of 3 real life music producers and 2 real life choreographers. They appear to us as digital models based on motion capture of the actual (human) members.

The Killing Voice shows these virtual members set against the usual KV Backdrop.  From left to right are Yejun, Bamby, Hamin, Noah, Eunho.  They have diverse vocals across many music genres.

K-POP SONG REQUEST POST — MARCH 2026
Tim Welch Vocal Studio

March 1

Edit

Hey K-Pop Crew!

February was MASSIVE. Before we dive into March requests, a few updates:

A warm welcome 🤝...to all the new REVELUV who came over after the Red Velvet Killing Voice — WELCOME! And to all the ATINY, InSomnia, NSWER, ENGENE, STAY, ARMY, MOA, ONCE, BLINK, who keep growing this community every week as well.

Be sure to add your requests TO THIS POST for MARCH👇 as comments below and jump into the K-POP chats on PATREON and DISCORD! You requesting them keeps them on my radar both for deep diving here on PATREON and for public facing videos on YouTube.

LOTS OF NEW COLLECTIONS: Stray Kids, Ateez, Enhypen & BTS now have specific collections THANKS TO YOU ALL being such engaged and organized ambassadors for them in my community.

🔥 FEBRUARY PROMISES — DELIVERED Remember my "I SEE YOU" list from last month? Here's where we landed:

✅ Red Velvet Killing Voice — multiple requesters, DONE! (84 minutes!)

✅ Red Velvet Backstory Reaction — DONE!

✅ Stray Kids "ESCAPE" — Savallion, DELIVERED! You waited since OCTOBER.

✅ Seungmin "Birds of a Feather" — Kagari, DONE!

✅ WOODZ "I'll Never Love Again" — Bennett, you requested this THREE MONTHS in a row. DELIVERED!

✅ Baekhyun "Ringa Ringa Ring" Live — Moon Flower, GOT IT!

✅ BTS "Magic Shop" — blkrose_22, your story behind this one was beautiful. DONE!

✅ ENHYPEN "Fatal Trouble" Recording — heeseung, DELIVERED!

✅ ATEEZ "Cyberpunk" — Taylor & Knightly, 40-MINUTE DEEP DIVE!

✅ Dreamcatcher "Vision" — GreyScale & Zaphne, DONE!

✅ Dreamcatcher "JUSTICE" — Metalstryke!

✅ XG "THE CORE" Full Album — Roger!

Plus ENHYPEN "No Doubt" In the Studio, ENHYPEN "Shout Out" Olympic Song, Jay "Always" Bon Jovi Cover, Stray Kids "Venom," Stray Kids Recording Scene MEGAVERSE + Cover Me Deep Dive, ATEEZ Mingi "ROAR," Agust D, TAEMIN "MOVE," NMIXX "Blue Valentine," KATSEYE Lollapalooza Full Set, and more!

👀 I SEE YOU (Persistent Requests I'm Getting To!)

These are on my radar. No need to re-request — but if you want to keep hearting them, that helps!

🔜 SEVENTEEN Killing Voice — Bennett, Elodie, hannah jade — every single month. It's in the KV queue. It's happening.

🔜 Baekhyun "The Letter" — Nika, 25 hearts, MONTHS of patience. Coming.

🔜 BTOB Killing Voice — Next up in the KV queue!

🔜 Forestella — Zaphne AND Maria, I see you both. Multiple months requesting.

🔜 Taeyeon Killing Voice — Rye and others, noted. After current queue.

🔜 NCT Dream/NCT 127 Killing Voice — still in the queue.

🔜 TXT — Bennett mentioned making an intro video

🚨 BTS ARMY — HEADS UP BTS "ARIRANG" album drops March 20! I'll be reacting when it releases. If you want specific BTS content before then, drop requests below.

💡 A NOTE ON HOW REQUESTS WORK I save ALL past song request posts including heart counts. If I didn't get to a song this month but it's had consistent hearts for two or three months in a row, that ABSOLUTELY moves it up my list. So if you've recommended something and I haven't gotten to it yet — KEEP RECOMMENDING IT. Your persistence is not annoying. It's data. It helps me serve you better. Nika proved it with Baekhyun. WOODZ took Bennett three months — and it got done. It works.

📝 HOW TO REQUEST, BE SUPER RESPECTFUL OF EACH OTHER 🙏 This is all about celebrating artists.

LINKS: Leave a comment with the YouTube link.

❤️ HEART EACH OTHERS' COMMENTS: This is everything! There are SO many recommendations that I can't get to all of them. Heart the ones you want to see! PRO TIP: Find your fellow fans on Discord and coordinate — Patreon doesn't let you message each other directly.

SHOUTOUTS! I'll give you a shoutout in the intro if I pick your comment. If your request is getting lots of hearts, feel free to add a few words about the artist that I can read on your behalf. (Don't want a shoutout? Just say so!)

HOW LONG SHOULD IT BE? (optional) Let me know if you're thinking:

QUICK TAKE (5-7 min) — $5 tier

SONG ANALYSIS (15-30 min) — $5 tier

DEEP DIVE (30+ min) — $10 tier

Since Tim also seems to like context for songs - I wanted to add that this song was written in response to racist / extremely disrespectful comments made by photographers at the Met Gala when they first went together in 2024 (the first full kpop group to attend, if I have my facts right). 

It's a diss track through and through. Though I  don't think they have straight up said "the Met Gala is why we wrote the song", it features the clap that Bang Chan did to disengage from the photographers and the timing is right. I think it was mentioned in the HOP Intro video that Bounce Back was originally much more aggro and the Company asked them to tone it down with "younger audiences" in mind.

structured.name: cashette
structured.text:
Thomas Bergersen (fr Two Steps from Hell) - Sonera 

https://youtu.be/IRrNx0LNdaI?si=AIfT8qIJPlAO89wy

Sonera feels different because it doesn’t just sound epic… it feels personal, like it’s telling a story without actually spelling anything out. The vocals aren’t in a real language, so you just absorb the emotion instead of getting distracted by lyrics, and that makes it hit deeper. It’s the kind of song you put on when you want to feel something big and cinematic but still intimate at the same

A NOTE ON HOW REQUESTS WORK

I want to be transparent about this because it matters:

I save EVERYTHING. Every song request post, every heart count, every comment. When I see the same song or artist showing up month after month with consistent hearts, that tells me something. It tells me there's a community of people here who genuinely want that content.

So if you've recommended something and I haven't gotten to it yet — KEEP RECOMMENDING IT. Don't assume I've forgotten or that it's not going to happen. When I see a pattern of something consistently getting hearts across multiple months, that ABSOLUTELY influences what I prioritize.

Your persistence is not annoying. It's data. It helps me serve you better.

Hearts are one factor. I also consider:

Community diversity (making sure all our communities get love)

Artists I haven't covered yet (new discoveries are what keep this exciting)

Patterns I see in the chats and Discord

What's trending or just dropped

The more you engage — hearts, comments, showing up in the chats — the more your community stays on my radar. That's how this works. You are the ambassadors for your favorite artists here.

I'm requesting a BIG reaction this month: Bo Burnham's Inside, the whole ding dang thing. 

It's on Netflix, I haven't figured out if there's a way to link directly to Netflix content but I trust you'll be able to find it. Hello! I'd like to recommend another Japanese artist called 9lana (pronounced Kulana). I've seen many instances of her being compared to ado because of her singing style and range. In fact, apparently the 9 in 9Lana refers to how she is able to sing in 9 different voices. She's a relatively new artist, who's debut was in 2023. I haven't seen anyone else recommend her yet, so here are my song recommendations for her:

Propose - 9lana cover of this song is amazing and demonstrates a lot of her vocal technique. I recommend starting from this song.
https://youtu.be/XPLkkdMFmco?si=-UZR55nCzHNvC9WK

Let Me Battle - This was 9lana's debut song produced by Giga and TeddyLoid, who also made some of Ado's songs such as Odo and Show. It was then used as the intro to popular anime series Pokémon.
https://youtu.be/whTcBozYrOo?si=idbj6XdKF6LE0wEr

Midnight parade - This is her most recent single and is one of my favourites. Lyric by 9Lana and Tana.H
Music by Tana.H and Marchin
https://youtu.be/_zJQAEdwJ9g?si=Uq_kcRB1wA5vKbmA

Other songs that are great and certainly worth checking out:

Balalaika: https://youtu.be/S2Xu6_XWy4M?si=i4M9edIMjH_V-1t5
Chinchiro-rhythm: https://youtu.be/RqK3bGxeH98?si=JLKJyfl2v2Ox0u2G
Kyubi: https://youtu.be/l70B7oRYKoo?si=yL-_5IrvxTz7TbU5
Only Way: https://youtu.be/TGowEYYtKII?si=6HaXxBMo71CIYJ_J

This should be enough to keep you sated for a month even if you go down the 9Lana rabbit hole. I hope you at least check her out at some point. I'll be back to repost next month if I don't see anything. I think she's a very underrated artist and deserves more attention. Love your content!

I am going to post this again, , it's a bit of a funny different thing. But I would love a reaction to Hololive's Omaru Polka' performance of her "Po" song at last year's 6th Holofes.

I am mostly intrigued by how, what (I think) is essentially a very simple song, is perceived by a professional as it is very fun and hype. But what is something that a layperson like me would not realise about the performance?

Last time I linked to a Clippers cut of the live, but here is the actual live video:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4C3kreEOk1Q&list=RD4C3kreEOk1Q&start_radio=1&t=4061s

Her performance starts around the 41.28 minute mark.

a little bit about Holofes, shamelessly stolen from the Hololive Fan Wiki:

"HoloFes concerts features nearly all Hololive members aside from ones that was then recently debuted. Songs selection contain a mixed of cover songs as well as originals. Every featured members have their own solo segment as well as group songs. The event would take place at a real venue with a paid live stream as well a free live preview on the Hololive main channel on Youtube. 1st Fes was originally a single day event but the number of talents led to 2nd Fes onwards being organized as a multi-day event with the performing talents being splits between those days. Starting with 3rd Fes, Holofes was organized alongside HoloExpo. Each fes, Cover also make changes to the presentation, starting with AR integration in 2nd Fes, full virtual cameras for stream viewers in 3rd Fes, Guest artist Stage in 4th Fes and three screen setup at the venue for 5th Fes.

According to Yagoo, 1st Fes was the turning point for Hololive Production as a whole and helped secured the investment to expand its operation including the funding for its billion yen multimedia studio."

About this particular performance, Omaru Polka is from Hololive JP 5th generation, and it is part of the free experience; most of the concert is paid for either in person or livestream. Overall, it is just really a fun performance, and had the responsibility of hyping up the crowd on the second day, as Suisei did on the first day with Stellar Stellar. 

Hololive 7th Fes is happening 6-8th of march :) 

Probably most suited for a quick check rather than a very deep analysis. 


For a longer video, you could consider extending it to the next two songs, as well as including Hakos Baelz, also known as "Bae" from Hololive English Promise. Who is known as an exceedingly great dancer. The third song would then be Houshou Marine from Hololive JP 3rd generation, who is the most subscribed active Hololive member, who performs in the Cube. 

Both of the two other songs are very different and more serious than the first one.

Recommending this since the VOD hosting expires after March 15 
https://akasupa.com/events/from-minto-with-love

Mint Fantome had her Valentines Live Concert. She performed 2 full jazz concerts back to back live 3d singing alongside a live band (the LA Diamond Trio), to a packed lounge, and prerecorded musical guests.  In the interest of time, I have timestamps in case people are interested in specific performers or songs from the set so feel free to comment if any of them are standouts to you:

Guest Performances:
Miori Celesta (6:55 I've Got You Under My Skin by Frank Sinatra)
Nimi Nightmare (17:25 Fly Me to the Moon)
Her group members, Phoebe Chan and Victoria Roman (23:00 Honeymoon Un Deux Trois by DATEKEN)
Sameko Saba in her first concert performance (37:45 Beyond the Sea by Robbie Williams)

0:35 Ain't That a Kick in the Head by Dean Martin
4:35 Love Me As Though There Were No Tomorrow by Nat King Cole
11:30 Solitude by Billie Holiday
14:30 Misty by Ella Fitzgerald
28:25 Can’t Say Goodbye to Yesterday by Carla White
34:45 A Thousand Dreams Of You by Leslie Cheung
43:50 Falling Behind by Laufey
48:00 A Kiss to Build a Dream On by Louis Armstrong
Encore:
51:45 Snake Eater by Cynthia Harrell

She debuted her new song from the composer of Snake Eater (55:15 Yet I'll Be With You) 
and a jazz rendition of her other original song, 1:00:30 Welcome to Mint Cafe (Jazz Arrange by StarlightDaryl). 

Tickets sold out in less than 2 minutes to a core crowd accustomed to more idol and J-pop performances so you might notice some more unusual shows of support.  We definitely surprised the band too who did amazing going along with the shenanigans.  Even crazier, due to circumstances they were only able to rehearse for an hour.

This was a real treat and a very impressive feat of indie ingenuity presented by Oshispark
Hope anyone into vtubing and fully live performances gets a chance to see it.

Jeff actually had quite the career with him even stopping music in between after changing record labels multiple times. I'd say his first big breakthrough was after the show kinnporsce got world famous and since he had written a song on set teh producers liked that became the OST! He then went on to perfom why don't you stay(https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6ZF8RXvV9sQ&list=RD6ZF8RXvV9sQ&start_radio=1) in multtiple places bec the kinnporsche cast went on a worldour to thank fans and perform for them! Also it is in english while the original song is in Thai ! Jeff rewrote it for this occasion and some people like to say the diff vers are the loveinterest (from the drama) viewpoints. Another thing I want to mention for Rain Wedding he didn't write the lyrics since they are as far as I know in the northern thai dialect ince the movie takes place in the north of Thailand. Sorry, this is kind of long.I just got really excited seeing you mention  him since i think he deserves a lot of love! Ayreon - "The Day That the World Breaks Down"
https://youtu.be/oFuMKdrzPqU?si=JoEA5LRMXRK9vh3J

Ayreon is the music project of Dutch musician/song writer/producer Arjen Lucassen. He does most of the songwriting and plays many instruments on his tracks but is famous for bringing in other musicians and singers for all of his projects. This album features 11 different singers, all of which are featured in this song, who all play a role within the story that is told through album.

Most of them are singers from rock and metal bands in Europe and I will gladly provide a list of the personnel but I'd rather wait until after you do reaction, if this makes it to that point.

What's great about this is he adds a lot of behind the scenes commentary about this album, the singers, and the story. 

There's a wide range of voices between all the singers that will give you a lot to break down and talk about. Hopefully this is something people will vote for and you can get to it some point.

3mo

 Thomas Bergersen (fr Two Steps from Hell) - Sonera 

https://youtu.be/IRrNx0LNdaI?si=AIfT8qIJPlAO89wy

Sonera feels different because it doesn’t just sound epic… it feels personal, like it’s telling a story without actually spelling anything out. The vocals aren’t in a real language, so you just absorb the emotion instead of getting distracted by lyrics, and that makes it hit deeper.
