structured.name: blkrose_22
structured.text: Magic Shop by BTS. This one is very special to me. Magic Shop is a technique used in therapy using the concept of trading negative for positive. BTS frame it as a mental refuge. A safe place for self healing by exchanging negative emotions and fear for positive emotions. I was having a hard time, on antidepressants and everything. This song is what broke through the mental block. I’m going to give two links. The first one is to a live performance, but the audience can hinder the audio at times.

K-POP SONG REQUEST POST — FEBRUARY 2026
Tim Welch Vocal Studio

February 1

Edit

Hey K-Pop Crew!

What a month January was. Before we dive into February requests, a few updates:

A warm welcome 🤝...

...to all the new ATINY and ENGENE who have come over to the channel recently, WELCOME! Be sure to add your requests to this post as comments below and jump into the K-POP chats on PATREON and DISCORD! You requesting them keeps them on my radar both for deep diving here on PATREON and for public facing videos on Youtube.

🔥 JANUARY PROMISES — DELIVERED

Remember my "I SEE YOU" list from last month? Here's where we landed:

✅ ATEEZ Killing Voice — Bennett, DONE! (70 minutes!)

✅ ENHYPEN Killing Voice — Kay, DELIVERED! (69 minutes!)

✅ Stray Kids "Thunderous" — Sophie, JUST DROPPED! (27-min analysis)

✅ Dreamcatcher — Metalstryke, first reaction posted!

Magic Shop by BTS. This one is very special to me. Magic Shop is a technique used in therapy using the concept of trading negative for positive. BTS frame it as a mental refuge. A safe place for self healing by exchanging negative emotions and fear for positive emotions. I was having a hard time, on antidepressants and everything. This song is what broke through the mental block. I’m going to give two links. The first one is to a live performance, but the audience can hinder the audio at times. structured.text: I don't know if you're still looking for anime to watch but I'd like to suggest Samurai Champloo. It's created by Shinichirō Watanabe (who also created Cowboy Bebop). It's set during the Edo period, ~1601-1868, but adds a modern spin to Japanese tradition and history with anachronistic hip hop elements as well as featuring hip hop on the soundtrack. It's not too long, the whole show is one session, 26 episodes. If you do give this show a watch, keep an eye (ear?) out for the traditional Okinawan

structured.name: Kimchi
structured.text:
Don't say I didn't warn you ‼️
It's about time you discover Taeyeon, the vocalist dubbed the 'Voice of Korea'. She is a top-tier female singer today, standing alongside IU.
She is the leader of South Korea's national girl group named Girls' Generation, and she is Baekhyun's one and only girlfriend.
I want you to open your mind to her 'Killing Voice' via this link: https://youtu.be/5ch94AaPZRQ?si=_t9DHkVnBpqGHSC0
And her group's video: https://youtu.be/6pA_Tou-DPI?si=u6CeqDtPh9mYtXoH"

structured.name: WarpSkaven
structured.text:
Not as active these days, but Steam-Powered Giraffe is one of my old faves. Small group from San Diego that were trained in pantomime, with a very unique aesthetic as automatons and put on incredible shows with a Vaudeville vibe
Incredible vocal ranges and very quirky but worth a listen...they have number of albums but their most well-known video is 'Honeybee'
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ojYK6CW8gdw

structured.name: Feli
structured.text:
Would you be interested in checking out a Chinese vocalist? His name is Charlie Zhou, 周深 (Zhou Shen).  

Zhou Shen (周深) first appeared on Chinese television in 2014 when he competed as a contestant on the third season of the reality singing competition The Voice of China (中国好声音). His voice doesn’t sit neatly in typical male/female vocal expectations, which makes his sound immediately striking.

This is his first tv appearance on The Voice at 21 years old. https://youtu.be/l9K5NLhYzzw?si=CYq_8hmV

structured.name: Sammi Knapp
structured.text: I know you enjoyed both Ado’s and IronMouse’s Aishte Aishte Aishte. You should try Nerissa Revencroft’s English Translation version “Love Me, Love Me, Love Me”. I think hearing it in English will give you a different perspective on the song. Plus help introduce you to Nerissa’s powerhouse of a voice. I know you heard her some when she was in IronMouse’s Clair Obscure, but Nerissa’s voice is just so amazing it would be a shame for you to miss out.

FEBRUARY SONG REQUEST POST + January Wrap-Up 🔥
Tim Welch Vocal Studio

February 1

Edit
Hey everyone!
SONG REQUESTS FOR FEBRUARY BELOW 👇
💥K-POP FEBRUARY 2026 SONG REQUEST POST 👉HERE

Please post all SONG REQUESTS (both long-form and single song ideas) as comments below on this post. ❤️ Heart each others' comments to increase the probability I'll get to them.

$10 TIER CREW

I LOVED YOUR IDEAS from the post I did👉 HERE about a week ago asking for more long-form ideas, lots of great stuff coming from that, but still feel free to double-down and share those ideas again on this post here to drum up even more interest. I have the results of that post saved though. 👍

(we have such a big K-POP community now that you all have earned your own monthly post!)

✅ SAVED PAST REQUESTS

I HAVE ALL PAST SONG REQUEST POSTS SAVED INCLUDING HEART COUNTS. Still share all of your song request ideas even if you requested it before, it still helps! For instance, if I didn't get to a song this month, but it's had 10 hearts on it two months in a row, you know I'm making it HIGH PRIORITY for the following month. Make sense?

A warm welcome 🤝...

...to all the new ATINY and ENGENE who have come over to the channel! Be sure to add your requests to the K-POP MONTHLY SONG REQUEST POST. Your support and engagement keeps them on my radar! 👉 HERE

🚨 KEEP AN EYE ON THE CHATS!

Sometimes I pull ideas from there in addition to the song request posts, and I also post ALL NEW RELEASES in the corresponding genre chats both on PATREON and DISCORD the moment they drop.

February song request posts are coming shortly, but first — I wanted to get some promises cleared before asking for more! Here's what got DONE:

✅ PROMISES DELIVERED

From my January "I SEE YOU" list:

✅ ATEEZ Killing Voice — Bennett, DONE! (70-min deep dive)

✅ ENHYPEN Killing Voice — Kay, DELIVERED! (69-min full analysis)

✅ Stray Kids "Thunderous" — Sophie, JUST DROPPED! (27-min analysis)

✅ Dreamcatcher — Metalstryke, first reaction posted!

✅ Home Free — Ms. Chuck Terry, Ring of Fire is up!

✅ Les Friction "Louder Than Words" — Cricket, YOUR PATIENCE WON 🏆

Plus coming THIS WEEK:

💥 New reactions for ATEEZ and ENHYPEN coming THIS WEEK! Welcome to all supporters of these two AMAZING groups who have come over. Lots of new people have joined us.

🔜 Stray Kids "ESCAPE" — Savallion, you've waited since OCTOBER. It's filmed. Coming in days.

🔜 Seungmin "Birds of a Feather" cover — Kagari, three months of patience about to pay off. Filmed and ready.

SB19 More to come!

🔥 JANUARY WAS A MASSIVE MONTH— Here's What You Might Have Missed
🇰🇷 K-POP

ATEEZ (ATINY) — Welcome to the family!

ATEEZ Killing Voice DEEP VOCAL ANALYSIS (1:09:27) — $10

Beginner's Guide to ATEEZ — Thanks Bennett!

ATEEZ Lemon Drop + ARRIBA + DJANGO Quick Take

ENHYPEN (ENGENE) — You showed up and we delivered!

ENHYPEN Killing Voice FULL ANALYSIS (1:09:38) — $10

ENHYPEN Backstory Reaction

Stray Kids (STAY)

Stray Kids "Thunderous" Song & Vocal Analysis (27:01) — JUST POSTED!

Stray Kids Lollapalooza Concert Part 3 — $10 tier trilogy complete!

Megaverse Song & Vocal Analysis

CASE 143 Analysis

I.N "Hallucination" Analysis

Hollow / THE FIRST TAKE Quick Take

2 more already filmed!

More K-Pop:

Taemin "Danger" Analysis — Ashley's intro video was 🔥

NMIXX "SPINNIN' ON IT" Vocal Analysis — WalkLikeAHuman keeps them on our radar!

Dreamcatcher "JUSTICE" Quick Take — So different, loved it!

🇵🇭 P-POP (A'TIN & Blooms!)

SB19 "CRIMZONE" Wish USA Bus — Ken's low notes! 😳

SB19 "Quit" Live Song & Vocal Analysis — This one got me emotional

SB19 "Bazinga" Quick Take

BINI "Cherry On Top" Quick Take Blooms...still here!? 🙌

🎮 VTuber (THE SWARM HAS ARRIVED!)

Neuro-sama "Life" Song & Vocal Analysis — The Beacon is lit, what do you all want next?

Neuro-sama "Colorful Array" Analysis — Existential thoughts about consciousness 🤯

Monii "Life" Cover — She co-wrote the original!

😈 Hazbin Hotel / Animation (Sinful Saturdays!)

Hazbin Hotel PILOT Full Reaction — Going up on YouTube!

Hazbin Hotel "It Starts with Sorry" Song Analysis

Hazbin Hotel "Whatever It Takes" Vocal Analysis

🗡️ Anime (Attack on Tuesday!)

Attack on Titan S1 Episode 18 — $10 tier, every Tuesday!

Sayuri "It's Like a Little Light" (ERASED)

🇹🇷 Manifest (New Community!) Any of you still here!? Keep them on my radar with song requests! 🙌

Manifest "Zamansızdık" Full Vocal Analysis

Manifest "RÜYA" Quick Take

Manifest "Snap" Quick Take

The Controversial Concert Discussion

🎸 Rock / Metal / The Warning

The Warning "Escapism" Honda Stage Analysis — JUST POSTED!

The Warning "Burnout" Sofar London

Nightwish "Weak Fantasy" Live — Gaye, this was for you!

Tarja "Song To The Moon" — She is the REAL DEAL

Les Friction "Louder Than Words" — Cricket, FINALLY!

MCR "Welcome to the Black Parade" — Blocked on YT, full version here!

Måneskin "Beggin'"

The HU "Wolf Totem" — Mongolian throat singing!

🎭 Musical Theatre

Sara Bareilles & Rufus Wainwright "She Used to Be Mine"

Phantom of the Opera "Think of Me" Celebrate & Compare — $10 tier

Lots of AMAZING musical recs to watch, going over those requests over the next couple days! So many I want to check out with you all.

🎮 Video Game Music

HEARTSTEEL "PARANOIA" ft. Baekhyun

Zenless Zone Zero "Burnice" Character Demo

🌍 International

Aldous Harding "Imagining My Man" — Real human singing raw YES!

Faouzia "Don't Ever Leave Me"

Alis "S'ta kam than" — Albania Eurovision 2026!

KATSEYE Lollapalooza Full Set Any KATSEYE fans here!?!

💬 YOUR COMMUNITIES ARE GROWING

We welcomed so many new faces in January:

ATINY — You came for the Killing Voice and many of you stayed! ATEEZ is here to stay on my radar.

ENGENE — ENHYPEN content converting like crazy. More coming.

The Swarm — Neuro-sama fans, you've built something special here. VTuber chat is thriving!

Manifest fans — These Turkish queens aren't going anywhere. Your support matters.

Every community here has a home. When I react to someone, I'm committing to them. Your continued engagement keeps them in my rotation — both here AND on YouTube.

📍 FIND YOUR PEOPLE

Patreon Chats:

🇰🇷 K-POP

🇯🇵 J-POP, J-ROCK & ANIME

🇵🇭 P-POP & SOUTHEAST ASIA

🇹🇷 EUROPE, TURKEY & MIDDLE EAST

🎮 VTUBER & VIDEO GAME MUSIC

🎭 MUSICAL THEATRE & SOUNDTRACKS

🎸 METAL & ROCK

Discord has dedicated artist channels for SB19, Stray Kids, The Warning, Ren, and Chappell Roan!

💥 FOR FREE MEMBERS

If you're reading this and haven't joined a paid tier yet —

$5/month gets you:

3x more content than YouTube

Quick Takes and Song Analyses daily

Blocked reactions that can't go on YouTube

Song request posts where YOUR vote matters

Chat access to find your people

$10/month gets you:

Everything above PLUS

Killing Voice deep dives (ATEEZ, ENHYPEN, Stray Kids, and more)

Full concert reactions (Lollapalooza, ACON, and more)

Anime episodes (Attack on Titan, Frieren)

Hazbin Hotel / Helluva Boss full episodes

70+ hours of long-form content and growing

📝 HOW TO REQUEST SONGS ON THIS POST

BE SUPER RESPECTFUL OF EACH OTHER 🙏 This is all about celebrating artists.

LINKS: Leave a comment with the YouTube link.

❤️ HEART EACH OTHERS' COMMENTS: This is everything! There are SO many recommendations that I can't get to all of them. Heart the ones you want to see! PRO TIP: Find your fellow fans on Discord and coordinate — Patreon doesn't let you message each other directly.

SHOUTOUTS! I'll give you a shoutout in the intro if I pick your comment. If your request is getting lots of hearts, feel free to add a few words about the artist that I can read on your behalf. (Don't want a shoutout? Just say so!)

HOW LONG SHOULD IT BE? (optional) Let me know if you're thinking:

QUICK TAKE (5-7 min) — $5 tier

SONG ANALYSIS (15-30 min) — $5 tier

DEEP DIVE (30+ min) — $10 tier

Your support is what makes all of this possible. It's what keeps YOUR favorite artists in my rotation.

Thank you for being here. Thank you for trusting me with your favorite artists. And thank you for holding me accountable to the things I promise you all!

I'm so excited about February, lots of exciting things cooking!

See you in the chats!
Tim

Posted February 1 at 11:24 AM

Hi Tim! :) I am once again recommending League songs!

Sherly Lee Ralph - Blood Sweat and Tears - https://youtu.be/rhTZTy1rZhw
This song was about Ambessa (from Arcane, but no spoilers!) when it was announced that she would be put into the game. The vocal texture in her voice is so juicy in this song.

Awaken - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zF5Ddo9JdpYI
was made to kick off the 2019 competitive season.

Legends Never Die - https://youtu.be/r6zIGXun57U 
is another REALLY GOOD song (practically iconic at this point). It was made for Worlds, which is their pro esports championship. They feature real artist Against the Current.

K/DA - MORE - https://youtu.be/3VTkBuxU4yk
Another K/DA song! Has the same singers (plus a new one!):
Madison Beer (playing Evelynn, the light purple haired singer with the glasses)
SOYEON (playing Akali, the rapper, from (G)I-DLE)
MIYEON (playing Ahri, the main singer with the fox tails/ears, from (G)I-DLE)
Jaira Burns (playing Kai'Sa, the black haired singer/choreographer)
Lexie Liu (playing Seraphine, the Chinese singer)

The Call - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mDYqT0_9VR4
was made to kick off the 2022 competitive season. They feature 2WEI, Louis Leibfried, and Edda Hayes.

I just put this in the chat too but the whole Oshi No Ko anime is very much worth a watch! Currently in the 3rd season and either this one will be the last or they will go for a 4th to wrap it up or a movie, so it won't be super long. It explores so much drama, politics, and social dynamics around idols and idol groups, acting and theater and TV, and involves a lot of music in the show in general. I think it would be a fascinating watch for you especially as you've been reacting to so much JPOP and KPOP!

I'm guessing you've been watching your anime on Crunchyroll- Oshi No Ko all seasons are available on HiDive or Amazon with HiDive add-on, and just season 3 is on Crunchyroll.

I don't know if you're still looking for anime to watch but I'd like to suggest Samurai Champloo. It's created by Shinichirō Watanabe (who also created Cowboy Bebop). It's set during the Edo period, ~1601-1868, but adds a modern spin to Japanese tradition and history with anachronistic hip hop elements as well as featuring hip hop on the soundtrack. It's not too long, the whole show is one session, 26 episodes. If you do give this show a watch, keep an eye (ear?) out for the traditional Okinawan song featured in a later episode, it's amazing how beautiful it is!

Ayreon - "The Day That the World Breaks Down"
https://youtu.be/oFuMKdrzPqU?si=JoEA5LRMXRK9vh3J

Ayreon is the music project of Dutch musician/song writer/producer Arjen Lucassen. He does most of the songwriting and plays many instruments on his tracks but is famous for bringing in other musicians and singers for all of his projects. This album features 11 different singers, all of which are featured in this song, who all play a role within the story that is told through album.

Most of them are singers from rock and metal bands in Europe and I will gladly provide a list of the personnel but I'd rather wait until after you do reaction, if this makes it to that point.

What's great about this is he adds a lot of behind the scenes commentary about this album, the singers, and the story. 

There's a wide range of voices between all the singers that will give you a lot to break down and talk about. Hopefully this is something people will vote for and you can get to it some point.

Crosspost from the recommendation channel, more detail there:

Mint Fantome, from the Snake Eater and Santa Baby reactions, just had her Valentines Live Concert. She performed 2 full jazz sets back to back live 3d singing alongside a live band (the LA Diamond Trio), to a packed lounge, and prerecorded musical guests:
Miori Celesta
Nimi Nightmare
Her group members, Phoebe Chan and Victoria Roman
Sameko Saba

Tickets sold out in less than 2 minutes to a core crowd accustomed to more idol and J-pop performances so you might notice some more unusual shows of support.  We definitely surprised the band too who did amazing going along with the shenanigans. She debuted her new song from the composer of Snake Eater and a jazz rendition of her other original song.  Even crazier, due to circumstances they were only able to rehearse for an hour.

This was a real treat and a very impressive feat of indie ingenuity presented by Oshispark
https://akasupa.com/events/from-minto-with-love
Since video recording or rebroadcasting the content to other users is prohibited, at least till March 16 (since that's when the vod availability ends), I can provide for a streaming ticket if Tim chooses to watch.  It just takes an account.  Hope anyone into vtubing and live performances gets a chance to check this out!

Tim Welch Vocal Studio

JANUARY 2026: What song should I check out next? ...(K-POP separate post)
Tim Welch Vocal Studio

January 1

Edit

Happy New Year!

Day one of the New Year has been a WILD ONE for me so far! I normally like to get these 'first of the month' messages out to you all first thing, but we had a water main break at the studio I teach out of in NJ THIS MORNING! Time to move studios...ugh! Lots of shuffling for me over the next few days with all of that but I should still be able to keep the content coming. 🤞

------------

K-POP SONG REQUEST 👉POST👈

🔥 IN CASE YOU MISSED IT...

if you're new here (welcome!) or haven't been keeping up, here's a few of many things I've posted on Patreon recently:

At the $5 tier: (single song analyses, quick takes)

SB19's full ACON 2025 performance

Ironmouse + MarcoMeatball Streamer Awards collab

Chainsaw Man's "KICK BACK" and "Jane Doe" deep dives

Kenshi Yonezu's "IRIS OUT" — the new Chainsaw Man opening

Fall Out Boy (iolanthe, your persistence paid off!)

WOODZ "Drowning" (Bennett & Maria, I heard you!)

Hazbin Hotel ON BROADWAY full reaction!

At the $10 tier: (longform analysis, deep dives, shows and anime,)

Stray Kids Lollapalooza concert reaction (Parts 2 released, & part 3 coming in the next couple days!)

Nightwish "The Greatest Show on Earth" deep analysis (24 minutes of symphonic metal glory — Gaye, this one was for you!)

Mamamoo Backstory & Killing Voice deep analysis

Hazbin Hotel full series (we're on Episode 3!)

Helluva Boss Season 2 through the FINALE

Attack on Titan full series (we're on Episode 13!)

IU Killing Voice reaction

EXO Killing Voice deep analysis

👀 I SEE YOU (Persistent Requests I'm Getting To!)

Some of you have been putting in WORK requesting the same songs for months. I want you to know — I see you, and these are coming soon. No need to re-request these:

Home Free — Ms. Chuck Terry, the acapella is coming

Les Friction "Louder than Words" — Cricket, your humble persistence wins

Galneryus "Angel of Salvation" — I said "Awesome, yes!" in chat and I meant it!

You don't need to heart these again — they're locked in. 🔒

📝 HOW TO REQUEST (Same Rules, Fresh Year!)

1. BE SUPER RESPECTFUL OF EACH OTHER 🙏 This is all about celebrating artists.

2. LINKS: Leave a comment with the YouTube link.

3. ❤️ EACH OTHERS' COMMENTS: This is everything! There are SO many recommendations that I can't get to all of them. Heart the ones you want to see! PRO TIP: Find your fellow fans on Discord and coordinate — Patreon doesn't let you message each other directly.

4. SHOUTOUTS! I'll give you a shoutout in the intro if I pick your comment. If your request is getting lots of hearts, feel free to add a few words about the artist that I can read on your behalf. (Don't want a shoutout? Just say so!)

5. HOW LONG SHOULD IT BE? (optional) Let me know if you're thinking:

QUICK TAKE (5-7 min) — $5 tier

SONG ANALYSIS (15-30 min) — $5 tier

DEEP DIVE (30+ min) — $10 tier

🚨 K-POP REQUESTS

We have such an incredible K-Pop community here that K-Pop gets its own post! This year I'm consolidating to ONE K-Pop request post — drop all your K-Pop requests (new groups AND existing artists) here:

👉 [K-POP SONG REQUESTS JANUARY 2026] 👈

Let's make 2026 even bigger. Drop those requests below and let's celebrate some amazing artists together!

Cheers, Tim

Posted January 1 at 1:58 PM

I would like to request another My Chemical Romance song, Vampires Will Never Hurt You. https://youtu.be/Gyu_hqpxj8M?si=AJ7V-bkPjKlZtF4P 

This was the  first song that they ever recorded and is still one of Gerard Way's favorite vocal performances that he has done.

There is a little bit of crazy backstory on this one since Gerard had a tooth access that day. He left in the middle of the session,  to get emergency dental work. He came back and was "woozy, slurring and emotionless." Due to the lack of budget, they needed to record that day. The other band members hid his pain medication, then, when he still wasn't delivering a good take, their friend and producer punched him in the mouth. This led to the very angry vocal take that Way gave in this song.

 Yorushika - That is Why I Gave Up on Music

This is from a Concert called Zense (Previous Life) which is an arrangement of their songs with "story readings" to tell a narrative of a woman meeting a man who recalls past lives.
Interestingly many of the songs from the concert, come from other narrative concept albums "That is Why I Gave Up On Music" and "Elma" (about a couple called Amy and Elma), and "Plagiarism" (about the rise, fall and healing of a plagiarist musical artist).

Ayreon is the music project of Dutch musician/song writer/producer Arjen Lucassen. He does most of the songwriting and plays many instruments on his tracks but is famous for bringing in other musicians and singers for all of his projects. This album features 11 different singers, all of which are featured in this song, who all play a role within the story that is told through album.

Most of them are singers from rock and metal bands in Europe and I will gladly provide a list of the personnel but I'd rather wait until after you do reaction, if this makes it to that point.

What's great about this is he adds a lot of behind the scenes commentary about this album, the singers, and the story. 

There's a wide range of voices between all the singers that will give you a lot to break down and talk about. Hopefully this is something people will vote for and you can get to it some point.

I was thinking of a suggestion: music from a video game that might catch your eye? There's a game called Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 that has been renowned recently for its soundtrack and has a TON of opera (opera-like but with trained singers?) in it with ultra rock flair along with classical music, a lot of it combined. Especially a final boss track called Une vie à t'aimer, which is very popular. 

Another theme called Lumière is very popular too, but it's less intense. It's written so that it's in various iterations of French throughout the centuries and such to make sure there's no spoilers since it's played at the beginning.  I'm not sure about that last part.

The former is epic. 

The composer was just some guy on Soundcloud that spent five years composing without knowing how crazy his music would rip in his spare time for an indie game he'd been randomly approached about. He got Best Score and Music and Best Soundtrack at the Gamer Awards and Steam Awards this year, respectively. Just a suggestion, but it'd be cool to see you break it down.

I'll add this over here. I'd consider it a perfect review for a long form YOUTUBE video. My reasons are in the post. 

Okay Tim, you might find this interessting. 

Here is Chappell doing a Live perfromance of several songs off of the School Nights EP in 2017, for Billboard Live. She is out supporting the School Nights EP that Atlantic crammed out while her album crawled to a standstill in production, and she was doing various shows. 

It includs Die Young (The Song I posted the video of a few days back),  and Bad for You, both on the School Nights EP, and Bitter which was unrealesed at the time, and planned for Atlantics Version of CR1 that never happened, though they did toss it out as a single in the next year. 

She has only got a single guitar backing her up. And she knocks out a flawless recording perfect version of all three songs. It's the same Chappell we know today on the raw skill level, just singing in the different cursive style, and oh so very young. Seven years before she'd break out in 2024. 

It's a great performance, and it feel it would be perfect for a YouTube long form review. Atlantic has had no cares about enforcing any copyrights on any of the School Nights material for years, (they seem a bit... bitter in fumbling her) and you could use it to highlight young Chappells skills in contrast to who she is today and how they all show here. It might be very instructive for some audience members and fun for you, as it would be three 'new songs' in a way, from her for you to look at. 

If you decide that you want to do this, and I'll write up a fast history of Chappell's School Nights era with Atlantic that you could open the video with to give viewers context. Many modern fans of hers are totally unaware of the School Nights EP, and other material from this time. 

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bHr2LNGU8lg

Virgil_dendrict

·

5mo

A new vtuber named Victoria Roman released a bilingual character song on debut that's a really fun mix of Enka and Rock to show off her skills. I thought the style is different enough it could be fun to analyze.  She's been studying under an Enka master for quite a long time now so I think she's really interested in getting more people into the Japanese genre. She also made the video to go along with it herself and is in a completely new group with Phoebe Chan and previous react video singer Mint Fantome
https://youtu.be/qpIH2VstEYU

structured.name: John
structured.text:
I always like to listen to people listen to Kim Jong Kook for the first time. A Korean Vocalist who debuted in 1995 as a Duo called Turbo before he became a soloist in 2001 after Turbo disbanded in 2000. K-POP SONG REQUEST POST JANUARY 2026
Tim Welch Vocal Studio

January 1

Edit

Hey all!

I've excited to keep our K-POP adventures continuing into 2026! We are going back to having just one post for K-POP song requests again and I included below in this post a link to all k-pop songs I've done on PATREON and Youtube.

👀 I SEE YOU (Persistent K-Pop Requests I'm Getting To!)

Some of you have been putting in WORK requesting the same songs for months. I see you, and these are coming soon. No need to re-request these:

ATEEZ Killing Voice — Bennett, I see you (and yes to that intro you offered!)

ENHYPEN Killing Voice — Kay, it's coming

Stray Kids "Thunderous" + "Cheese" — Sophie, your all-time faves are locked in

Stray Kids "ESCAPE" — Savallion, you've been asking since October, I got you

Seungmin's SONG by covers — Kagari, three months of patience is about to pay off

Dreamcatcher — Metalstryke, finally checking out that rock-influenced K-Pop!

You don't need to heart these again — they're locked in. 🔒

📝 HOW TO REQUEST (Same Rules, Fresh Year!)

1. BE SUPER RESPECTFUL OF EACH OTHER 🙏 This is all about celebrating artists.

2. LINKS: Leave a comment with the YouTube link.

3. ❤️ EACH OTHERS' COMMENTS: This is everything! There are SO many recommendations that I can't get to all of them. Heart the ones you want to see! PRO TIP: Find your fellow fans on Discord and coordinate — Patreon doesn't let you message each other directly.

4. SHOUTOUTS! I'll give you a shoutout in the intro if I pick your comment. If your request is getting lots of hearts, feel free to add a few words about the artist that I can read on your behalf. (Don't want a shoutout? Just say so!)

5. HOW LONG SHOULD IT BE? (optional) Let me know if you're thinking:

QUICK TAKE (5-7 min) — $5 tier

SONG ANALYSIS (15-30 min) — $5 tier

DEEP DIVE (30+ min) — $10 tier

I think I already suggested CASE 143! You mentioned rapping in your JJAM reaction, I would suggest MEGAVERSE.

CASE 143: I would say is a vibrant, pop-driven track that combines playful energy with a catchy, hook-heavy melody. The song’s concept revolves around love as a “mystery case,” blending humor, quirkiness, and relatable emotions. There is a music video, performance video, dance practice, Japanese version via The First Take, and color coded lyric video.

Music Video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jYSlpC6Ud2A&list=RDjYSlpC6Ud2A&start_radio=1
The First Take: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=g8h2KMnRQDo&list=RDg8h2KMnRQDo&start_radio=1

MEGAVERSE: I think displays a maximalist production style and emphasizes their rap precision. It is definitely high energy and can definitely be described as cinematic! There is a music video, dance practice video, color coded lyrics, and video making film.

Music Video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JqwPCzJnYyY&list=RDJqwPCzJnYyY&start_radio=1
Video Making: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JVQxGC-_x4E

Red Velvet Killing Voice Deep Dive
https://youtu.be/KEFNlx4ZIOU?si=LHc4ptI4g4NaacD2

You really enjoyed their Psycho performance, so I think you'd really enjoy doing a deeper dive into their discography.

One of my favorite things about them is their ability to produce dynamic soundscapes with their harmonies and interweaving ad libs. I think you'd have a blast analyzing their separate vocal tambres, techniques, and also music stylings. With their Killing Voice performance, I was able to hear some extra harmonies that are usually more in the background in their studio releases instead. 

Some fun factoids (mostly just what I've heard, so take with a grain of salt):
- It's one of the longest Killing Voice videos since their discography is massive. I've heard it was originally 40-45 min long, but had to be cut down. The final version is missing quite a few title tracks from their later releases. 

- It was recorded twice to accommodate the cut down version and also because they didn't like the level of their energy the first go.

- Yeri was sick during recording, so she's a little more subdued than the others.

I really hope you get a chance to check them out some more outside of Psycho! 🩷💛💚💙💜

I believe this is a new group to this channel. Some information about this group is that they are a project idol band which debuted in October of 2023 under Tamago Production. This band has four members known as Chodan (drummer), Magenta (bass), Siyeon (vocalist/guitarist), and Hina (guitarist/keyboard). This stage is their first music show performance which happened quite a bit after their debut, which is quite unusual in the kpop scene since many idol groups make their debut on these sorts of stages. structured.text:
Since we're heading into Christmas it would be fun to check out White Wine In The Sun by Tim Minchin. 
Since it’s summer in Australia Christmas songs don't really capture what the feeling of Christmas is like down here. I think White Wine In The Sun does a great job of doing just that.
I play it every year and every year it makes me cry.
https://youtu.be/fCNvZqpa-7Q?si=QXTPtVRnOINHHa0U

structured.name: Zephyr
structured.text:
Fun fact White Wine in the Sun is one of the reasons Tim got the job to write the songs for Matilda The Musical. The director for the musical went to see Tims comedy show to scout him out, however by the end of the night he decided Tim wasn't right for it as all of his songs were very dark, satirical and sweary, just not what they were looking for. 
However, Tim likes to have people leave his shows with a bit of emotional whiplash so for the encore he played White Wine in the Sun. While the song

December 2025: What song should I check out next?
Tim Welch Vocal Studio

Dec 1, 2025

Edit
🚨THIS POST IS FOR ALL SONG ANALYSIS REQUESTS OTHER THAN K-POP.

We have such a big K-POP community here that we now have separate posts!

🚨 K-POP: NEW (to me) GROUPS ARTISTS DECEMBER 2025👈

🚨 K-POP SONG REQUESTS DECEMBER 2025 (🚨not new groups)

COMMENTING GUIDELINES:

BE SUPER RESPECTFUL OF EACH OTHER: 🙏 This is all about celebrating artists.

LINKS: Leave a comment for the song including the link to the youtube video.

❤️ EACH OTHERS' COMMENTS: This is everything. There are typically so many recommendations that there's no way I will get to all of them. That's why it's important for the community to come together and 'uptick' ideas that are wanted by the collective by hearting others' ideas. (PRO TIP: 😀 Reach out to each other on Discord to get help with hearts on your comment if you find fellow fans of your favorite artists in our community. (PATREON doesn't allow you all to reach out to each other.)

SHOUTOUTS! I will give you a shoutout in the intro of the analysis if you are the one whose comment I chose. So, if it looks like your recommendation is likely to be picked based on the amount of ❤️'s on it, feel free to add on to the comment and say a few words about the group that I will read on your behalf. 🚨 If you don't want a shoutout just let me know in your comment too! 🙏

HOW LONG SHOULD IT BE?: (optional) Specify whether you think a QUICK TAKE (5-7 minute reaction) or SONG ANALYSIS (15-30 minute reaction,) or a DEEP DIVE ANALYSIS (30+ minute analysis) is appropriate for your request. * I can't guarantee I will always do a full song analysis of your reaction, but please specify anyway. QUICK TAKES and SONG ANALYSES under 30 minutes live at the $5 tier, while DEEP DIVES that are 30+ minutes live at the $10 long form tier.

So excited to check out some more music with you all and wrap 2025 together! 🎉🥳🎊

Cheers,

Tim

Posted Dec 1, 2025 at 6:36 AM

Hi Tim! :) I am once again recommending League songs!

Sherly Lee Ralph - Blood Sweat and Tears - https://youtu.be/rhTZTy1rZhw
This song was about Ambessa (from Arcane) when it was announced that she would be put into the game. The vocal texture in her voice is so juicy in this song.

Pentakill - Mortal Reminder - https://youtu.be/5-mT9D4fdgQ
Pentakill is their fictional metal group using champions from League. They partner with real artist Jorn Lande (who is the main singer and whose character is Karthus). There are other characters as well in the band: 
Olaf (drummer), 
Mordekaiser (guitarist), 
Yorick (bassist), 
Sona (keyboardist), 
and Kayle (other singer in different Pentakill songs, she is the one with the wings).

HEARTSTEEL - PARANOIA - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MDErQ1KTzaI
True Damage is their fictional boyband group using champions from League. They partner with real artists:
BAEKHYUN (playing Ezreal, the teal haired vocalist),
tobi lou (playing K'Sante, the bridge singer),
ØZI (playing Sett, the pink haired rapper from the fighting ring),
and Cal Scruby (playing Kayn/Rhaast, the first rapper with the pink/purple hair). 
There are also the characters Aphelios (the blue haired guy with the mask) who is the writer, and Yone (the white haired guy) who is their manager.

Awaken - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zF5Ddo9JdpYI
was made to kick off the 2019 competitive season.

Legends Never Die - https://youtu.be/r6zIGXun57U 
is another REALLY GOOD song (practically iconic at this point). It was made for Worlds, which is their pro esports championship. They feature real artist Against the Current.

K/DA - MORE - https://youtu.be/3VTkBuxU4yk
Another K/DA song! Has the same singers (plus a new one!):
Madison Beer (playing Evelynn, the light purple haired singer with the glasses)
SOYEON (playing Akali, the rapper, from (G)I-DLE)
MIYEON (playing Ahri, the main singer with the fox tails/ears, from (G)I-DLE)
Jaira Burns (playing Kai'Sa, the black haired singer/choreographer)
Lexie Liu (playing Seraphine, the Chinese singer)

Back with my Tom Cardy suggestion: Transcendental Cha Cha Cha
https://youtu.be/BLzxuIfD9rU?si=IIStuFpNp7d9-tyO

I love this song, it had me in an absolute chokehold when it came out. One of my favorite parts is the animated music video, it's done so well and really SHOWS the comedy in a way that I think would have been hard for him to accomplish with his usual skits. For a little background: Tom Cardy is known for short-form comedy songs. I think people write him off because he typically goes for SPEED and crude jokes over quality and he really leans into the "homemade tiktok video" aesthetic, but if you dig a little you can see it's a stylistic choice. When he goes all in, he really cooks.

It's hard to pick how long the reaction should be - this could be a quick take? But then, I feel like anything COULD be 😂

I would like to request another My Chemical Romance song, Vampires Will Never Hurt You. https://youtu.be/Gyu_hqpxj8M?si=AJ7V-bkPjKlZtF4P 

This was the  first song that they ever recorded and is still one of Gerard Way's favorite vocal performances that he has done.

There is a little bit of crazy backstory on this one since Gerard had a tooth access that day. He left in the middle of the session,  to get emergency dental work. He came back and was "woozy, slurring and emotionless." Due to the lack of budget, they needed to record that day. The other band members hid his pain medication, then, when he still wasn't delivering a good take, their friend and producer punched him in the mouth. This led to the very angry vocal take that Way gave in this song.

Since we're heading into Christmas it would be fun to check out White Wine In The Sun by Tim Minchin. 
Since it’s summer in Australia Christmas songs don't really capture what the feeling of Christmas is like down here. I think White Wine In The Sun does a great job of doing just that.
I play it every year and every year it makes me cry.
https://youtu.be/fCNvZqpa-7Q?si=QXTPtVRnOINHHa0U

Zephyr

·

6mo

Fun fact White Wine in the Sun is one of the reasons Tim got the job to write the songs for Matilda The Musical. The director for the musical went to see Tims comedy show to scout him out, however by the end of the night he decided Tim wasn't right for it as all of his songs were very dark, satirical and sweary, just not what they were looking for. 
However, Tim likes to have people leave his shows with a bit of emotional whiplash so for the encore he played White Wine in the Sun. While the song starts off funny it ends with a lot of heart and often leaves people in tears, which made the director change his mind.

From the Vtubing world: in November we had cool news from a group of vtubers you already glimpsed. Remember the 3-member group in the Music box of fate community edition? The comment should've taught you that they were an idol group who was supposed to be launched by Vshojo but got caught in the whole mess.  Well, now they've started their group on their own! It’s called Densetsu.exe and they already have a song out: 
Densetsu MVPs  ==> https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ExFMPZIK4pM

Also, every member made a song for the group introduction to the public. So in order Mint Fantôme song:
Welcome to Mint♡Cafe ==> https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=E2Y_Iv1P-t8

Victoria Roman (Vicky):
Night Parade ==> https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qpIH2VstEYU

Phoebe Chan:
Bloom Again ==> https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gnYYWwT2ziY

They're pretty awesome and I look forward to see what they'll get up to. Oh also, if you want to prep a bit, look up japanese maid cafe culture for Mint's song. It will help you understand it a bit more. And for Vicky's song, you could look up her singing style: Enka.

structured.text: Lastly, and possibly my favorite is V of BTS with Bing Crosby for White Christmas. It was a cross generational duet spearheaded by the Crosby estate. text:
Tim Welch Vocal Studio

Home

Posts

Collections

Chats

Shop

Membership

Recommendations

Edit page
K-POP SONG REQUESTS DECEMBER 2025 (🚨not new groups)
Tim Welch Vocal Studio

Dec 1, 2025

Edit

🚨 REQUESTS FOR NEW (to me) K-POP GROUPS/ARTISTS THAT YOU'D LIKE A DEEP DIVE OF GO HERE 👉K-POP: NEW (to me) GROUPS ARTISTS DECEMBER 2025👈

🚨 THIS POST is about finding SONGS by groups or artists that are already in my rotation, or artists that aren't yet in my rotation, but don't need a full deep dive first impression before reacting to them: like a backstory video and a Tiny Desk or Killing Voice etc.

(I was chatting with community member Bennett on Discord and he said there was so much discourse around who the next group deep dive should be that he requested we do a separate post just for that. Happy to give it a try this month, provided it doesn't become too confusing for the community to share ideas. Give feedback on that please. )

COMMENTING GUIDELINES:

BE SUPER RESPECTFUL OF EACH OTHER: 🙏 We have very passionate lovers of K-POP in our community, and I discourage engaging in "...no MY group is THE BEST..." type of communicating. That usually starts unnecessary drama, and I do not support that in my community. This is all about celebrating artists, and we can celebrate ALL of your favorites together.

LINKS: Leave a comment for the song including the link to the youtube video.

❤️ EACH OTHER: SUPER IMPORTANT! Put '❤️''s on others' comments! There are typically so many recommendations that there's no way I will get to all of them. That's why it's important for the community to come together and 'uptick' ideas that are wanted by the collective. (PRO TIP: 😀 Reach out to each other on Discord to get help with hearts on your comment if you find fellow fans of your favorite artists. PATREON doesn't allow you all to reach out to each other.)

SHOUTOUTS! I will give you a shoutout in the intro of the analysis if you are the one whose comment I chose. So, if it looks like your recommendation is likely to be picked, feel free add on to the comment and say a few words about the group that I will read on your behalf. 🚨 If you don't want a shoutout just let me know in your comment too! 🙏

HELP WITH NAMES: (optional) It's super helpful for me if you'd include the color coded lyric video which usually shows who is singing where in the group, so I can learn who the artists are better.

HOW LONG?: (optional) Specify whether you think a QUICK TAKE (5-7 minute reaction) or SONG ANALYSIS (15-30 minute reaction,) or a DEEP DIVE ANALYSIS (30+ minute analysis) is appropriate for your request. * I can't guarantee I will always do a full song analysis of your reaction, but please specify anyway. QUICK TAKES and SONG ANALYSES under 30 minutes live at the $5 tier, while DEEP DIVES that are 30+ minutes live at the $10 long form tier.

I'm having a blast, checking out K-pop with you all! So so so much more to come.

Cheers,

Tim

Posted Dec 1, 2025 at 6:14 AM

Quick Take - Cheese
https://youtu.be/YLtEc-kvOqA?si=LSmAQ0FJJIObL1dP

So I thought it would be fun to look at two Straykids songs from an album that you dont know yet which is "Noeasy". Noeasy was released in 2021 so about a year after Gods Menu. While Gods Menu was a huge breakthrough moment for them it also got them alot of hate and critic. People where claiming that their music was too "noisy" meaning that they are too loud, use too many weird sounds and just in general too unconventional. So this album was really them saying "you thought Gods Menu was too noisy? Well f u we made this one even noiser". Both Thunderous and Cheese play into that message (btw cheese also directly refernces Gods Menu with the DUDUDU part). Personaly I absolutly love both Songs they are very diffrent but also very Straykids and I thought doing both would give you a really good idea of what that album was all about😊 I also added a link to a live version (simular Format as the first takes) of Thunderous that im very fond of to give you some live vocals. But I would definatly recommand to watch the MV first bc its fire!!

Since someone got  Christmas EveL already dropped below I'm going to repeat Stray Kids - ESCAPE again. I just love this song so much and the video is like a short film, they act so well it really feels like a whole story.

It is a duo song with Bang Chan and Hyunjin (Hyunjin has the buzz cut in this video). It's described by them as an extension or sequel to "Red Lights". Where Red Lights was about sort of being trapped by compulsion / workaholic tendencies, Escape is... about escaping. Also my previous notes: Bang Chan is the one singing around 1:20, since it's not clear in the video and people generally ask (the first lyric video that comes up on YT is also wrong and includes an annotated correction). Also to help with understanding the video Bang Chan is often depicted as a wolf while Hyunjin is depicted as a weasel or ferret. 

This COULD be a quick take, sure, but dang, I would really love a more in depth one 😂 Dealer's choice.

To sum it up the video shows them directing the recording and giving tips to one another about how to sing certain parts in songs or other similar things and just basically see the process and how they do it as they are always very involved in it. Someone commented that this is a compilation and not as focused as other videos, which to be fair is true, but otherr videos are longer and I am not sure if you have the time to watch them. Besides, this compilation also serves as a sort of preview so you know what the others are like. If you you would like or prefer to watch the longer and more detailed videos (not compilations, but actual making of and filming of recording process of their albums, which you can find on Stray Kids youtube channel), I would love it as I think many stays would too. I just thought this would be more accessible for you as a first. Nonetheless, I really hope you consider giving this a chance, as it really shows how involved they are in making their music and also how passionate they are about it. After seeing your reaction to the live version of Stray Kids' S Class I wanted to drop it here. I don't think it's been recommended yet this month, but sorry if I'm stepping on toes the post gets so long 😂. It's just a really great Stray Kids song. Just like God's Menu it captures their unique flavor really well and the video is crazy. 

I'll include a link to the "making of" behind the scenes video if anyone wants to watch it because it shows how seriously they take performing even for the music videos. 5 days of filming, impressive use of practical special effects (so much fire??), not to mention having a fun time with each other doing little missions for fun, and being so gracious towards the staff and dancers and everyone helping out. 
This song is also really great on Beat Saber, just FYI.

S Class MV: https://youtu.be/JsOOis4bBFg?si=DmpqSp6VVGrS357q
S Class color-coded lyrics: https://youtu.be/EzgCa_m8pOY?si=yAevvL3-cPn7in1d

Lastly, and possibly my favorite is V of BTS with Bing Crosby for White Christmas. It was a cross generational duet spearheaded by the Crosby estate. They isolated his vocals and added V in an effort to keep his legacy alive. I think their voices blend together wonderfully. The dog in the video is Yeontan, V’s dog who we as ARMY got to see grow up from a tiny puppy, and we got the news that he had passed just a couple months before this video was released. https://youtu.be/r3QP1SxW1_I?si=GFHLUwiOxNZGY2Ae

Snooze (feat. Ryuichi Sakamoto, WOOSUNG of The Rose) by Agust D - a track that I’ve been really falling into this time of year, when stress can be so high at end of term.

Agust D (Suga of BTS) has said that Snooze is dedicated to aspiring younger artists, and I think it’s also become a comfort song for a lot of people (myself included). I really love Woosung’s voice in this as well, and the piano melody is from Ryuichi Sakamoto - one of the last projects he worked on before he passed. The video linked isn’t the official music video (there isn’t one as far as I’m aware) but it’s well done and the lyrics in the captions are good.

I'll probably transfer this breakdown to January too! But I think I already suggested CASE 143! You mentioned rapping in your JJAM reaction, I would suggest MEGAVERSE.

CASE 143: I would say is a vibrant, pop-driven track that combines playful energy with a catchy, hook-heavy melody. The song’s concept revolves around love as a “mystery case,” blending humor, quirkiness, and relatable emotions. There is a music video, performance video, dance practice, Japanese version via The First Take, and color coded lyric video.

Music Video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jYSlpC6Ud2A&list=RDjYSlpC6Ud2A&start_radio=1
The First Take: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=g8h2KMnRQDo&list=RDg8h2KMnRQDo&start_radio=1

MEGAVERSE: I think displays a maximalist production style and emphasizes their rap precision. It is definitely high energy and can definitely be described as cinematic! There is a music video, dance practice video, color coded lyrics, and video making film.

Music Video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JqwPCzJnYyY&list=RDJqwPCzJnYyY&start_radio=1
Video Making: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JVQxGC-_x4E

structured.name: Boop
structured.text: I would love to see AKMU! They have a killing voice video but I would love to see a deep dive into any of their music. They are a brother and sister duo that won a singing competition in 2012 with their original music. Early in their career, they were known more for their fun, whimsical lyrics and style. Now, they've matured their sound to tackle some darker concepts. The brother, Chan-hyuk, still writes and produces all of their music.

Dec 1, 2025

Edit

🚨 SONG REQUESTS FOR ARTISTS I'VE ALREADY BEEN INTRODUCED TO, GO HERE 👉K-POP SONGS DECEMBER 2025👈

🚨 This post is about finding the next GROUP or solo K-POP artist that I would do a deep dive on, including the backstory video and KILLING VOICE or Tiny Desk.

(I was chatting with community member Bennett on Discord and he said there was so much discourse around this topic that we needed a separate post for it. Happy to give it a try this month, provided it doesn't become too confusing for the community to share ideas. Give feedback on that please. )

This type of analysis typically lives at the $10 long form reaction tier as they are a much larger time investment, albeit a fun one!

COMMENTING GUIDELINES:

-BE SUPER RESPECTFUL OF EACH OTHER! 🙏 We have very passionate lovers of K-POP in our community, and I discourage engaging in "...no MY group is THE BEST..." type of communicating. That usually starts unnecessary drama, and I do not support that in my community. This is all about celebrating artists, and we can celebrate ALL of your favorites together.

-Leave a comment for the group/artist including any BACKSTORY videos, Killing Voice or Tiny Desk links.

-SUPER IMPORTANT! Put '❤️''s on others' comments! There are typically so many recommendations that there's no way I will get to all of them. That's why it's important for the community to come together and 'uptick' ideas that are wanted by the collective. (PRO TIP: 😀 Reach out to each other on Discord to get help with hearts on your comment if you find fellow fans of your favorite artists. PATREON doesn't allow you all to reach out to each other.)

-I will give you a shoutout in the intro of the analysis if you are the one whose comment I chose. So, if it looks like your recommendation is likely to be picked, feel free add on to the comment and say a few words about the group that I will read on your behalf. 🚨 If you don't want a shoutout just let me know in your comment too! 🙏

I'm having a blast, checking out K-pop with you all! So so so much more to come.

Cheers,

Tim

Posted Dec 1, 2025 at 5:46 AM

Hello all! I have been LOVING live-streaming with you all, and I planned on doing A TON of live streaming this weekend with watching the whole Eurovision finals a bit later, as well as a stream of Ren's saga 'The Tales' on Sunday. But I'm dealing with some family health issues this weekend and I unfortunately need to cancel today and tomorrows' live-streams. I am so sorry about that for those of you that planned on joining me. WHAT I'LL DO INSTEAD Eurovision 2026 If there is interest, I'll break down whatever Eurovision performances you'd like me to after the fact. I can also do some deeper study here in the community as well as some public facing stuff on YouTube of your favorites. I know there were several past Eurovision songs you all wanted me to check out as well so maybe we could do a whole live-stream around that? I'm definitely open, just can't do this weekend. Ren 'The Tales' Renegades, do we still want to live-stream this together? I would be TOTALLY down to do that, I didn't get very much interest in it in the comments or from the community, so I'd like to see if it is something we want to reschedule, or if that might not be the right fit at this time? If the live-stream doesn't feel like the right fit, I'm happy to break down the songs from the saga that I haven't heard yet. Thank you for understanding 🙏 AND...thank you SO MUCH for supporting the channel and being a part of this journey with me. I'm having a blast listening to all of this amazing music with you all. So much more to come! Cheers, Tim

OR3O & Toast) ✅ 5 VTuber Moments That Blew My Mind ✅ Finland Eurovision: Linda Lampenius ✅ Raye "I Know You're Hurtin" ✅ SEVENTEEN Killing Voice ✅ BTS "Hooligan" ✅ DISCOVERING FUJII KAZE (5-song bundle) ✅ Alexandra Căpitănescu "Choke Me" 🇷🇴 Romania Eurovision That's over 45 reactions, analyses, concerts, episodes, and albums since April 1. Thank you for your participation in the community with comments on the videos and in the chats! I'm so grateful we have grown so much and have such a supportive community. You all are the absolute BEST birthday gift I could've wished for. Tim 🙌

structured.name: Jamie Wirth
structured.text:
One more rock/metal suggestion. 

Bad Omens - "Specter"
They're one of my favorite bands of the last couple years. Very melodic, emotional,  rock/metal. This song features some great vocals from their singer, Noah Sebastian, who has a very emotive voice. He had good range and can tap into a number of different styles and textures. And this song has some great production in general. 

https://youtu.be/EUe9UhcUtEI?si=i9pEohbnWNduXYFw

structured.name: Jamie Wirth
structured.text:
Rock/Metal suggestion:

Novelists - Okapi (Live + Gospel version).

While they are classified as metal. Many of their songs are very melodic and even have some pop elements. They are not extreme enough that the average listener wouldn't still enjoy them. 

Them song in particular is one of the most beautiful metal songs I've ever heard. The singer's vocals were captured live on site where the video was recorded. It was then mixed with her own backing vocals along with the rest of the album track

K-POP SONG RECOMMENDATION GO 👉 HERE NOW. Woo hoo!

*We've had such a wonderful influx of K-POP AMBASSADORS in the community that I felt it's best to create a complete separate post for that community. I am LOVING my adventures with you all, so many exciting things coming for November in the world of K-POP and K-POP adjacent artists.

NEW COLLECTION!👇

Reactions to Nordic Artists Collection

I realized we have a big representation of AMBASSADORS for Nordic artists include, Aurora, Ghost, Nightwish, Laufey and so many more that I decided to make a separate collection just for those reactions. I'm also considering creating a separate chat for this category as well since it is so popular over here. If you have thoughts on that, let me know.

OCTOBER, WOW!

What an amazing month October was with you all, we really grew a lot as a community over here, and checked out some incredible music and content.

As always, I'm loving the positive energy from everyone as well as the inclusivity I am seeing. It's seriously heartwarming, thank you so much.

Can't wait to get to listening to more music with you all, and as always, don't hesitate to send me ideas on how I can make this a more fulfilling, entertaining and educational experience for you all.

See you in the chats!

Tim

Sherly Lee Ralph - Blood Sweat and Tears - https://youtu.be/rhTZTy1rZhw

Awaken - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zF5Ddo9JdpYI

Legends Never Die - https://youtu.be/r6zIGXun57U

I'll put my plea in this message as well in case you see it first: it's savaLLion, not savayon 😂 You do pronounce the Ls, like Medallion. Or just shortening to "Sava" is fine too, a lot of my friends do that. 

I'm actually going to suggest something that ISNT stray kids for once (wow). This song had me in an absolute chokehold when it came out: Transcendental Cha Cha Cha by Tom Cardy. 

All the songs on this album were certified BANGERS, but this one was accompanied by an incredible fully-animated music video that is just. SO. GOOD. If you hadn't heard of him, Tom Cardy does a lot of silly comedic songs for YouTube/TikTok but he also creates some really polished, smart pieces like this. If you like this one there are a lot of other really good ones as well, some being a bit niche.

Case 143 by StrayKids. 

You mentioned finding it unique in the killing voice and I have recently rediscovered my love for the song. 

It is an energetic, glitchy, pop-rap track that treats falling in love like catching an unexpected “case.” It mixes bold rap, catchy hooks, and chaotic sound effects to capture the confusing, overwhelming rush of new feelings

Carryover from last month: Stray Kids ESCAPE. This is a duo song with Bang Chan and Hyunjin, described by them as a sequel or extension of Red Lights. Lots of little references to the Red Lights video if you can remember it. 

This is pretty solidly one of my favorite songs, period, and the video is so cinematic and tells a powerful story. Their acting is so good here, too. To help with understanding some of the symbolism: Bang Chan is usually associated with being a wolf, while Hyunjin is associated with being a ferret. (all the stray kids have a representative animal).

Easy to tell them apart in this one because Hyunjin had his buzz cut here. Also since it's not super clear from the video - Bang Chan is the one singing around 1:20 - a lot of people ask who it is singing during that part.

https://youtu.be/LvCmsOWPwKA?si=f4e09UVI29q1Y04k

On last month recommendation post I had suggested this video, despite it not being a music video. Again, I am not sure if I can suggest it since it's not exactly just a song by a group, but clips of the process of recording the albums (so technically songs do appear, kinda). To sum it up the video shows them directing the recording and giving tips to one another about how to sing certain parts in songs or other similar things and just basically see the process and how they do it as they are always very involved in it. Someone commented that this is a compilation and not as focused as other videos, which to be fair is true, but otherr videos are longer and I am not sure if you have the time to watch them. Besides, this compilation also serves as a sort of preview so you know what the others are like. If you you would like or prefer to watch the longer and more detailed videos (not compilations, but actual making of and filming of recording process of their albums, which you can find on Stray Kids youtube channel), I would love it as I think many stays would too. I just thought this would be more accessible for you as a first. Nonetheless, I really hope you consider giving this a chance, as it really shows how involved they are in making their music and also how passionate they are about it. Really been loving your stray kids song reactions. 
https://youtu.be/BkZrKyjBeIk?si=Gd1-LewR_f1KU97F
My first language isn't english, so please do tell me if something didn't make sense 😊

Before my rec: its SavaLLion, you pronounce the L's, like medallion 😂 or just "Sava" is also perfectly fine. 

I'll do my recs separate again so people can heart the specific one(s) they want. First up is the Stray Kids version of "Lose My Breath". This is fully English. The song is from a collab they did with Charlie Puth. The official song/music video that includes Charlie Puth is excellent, but this "Stray Kids" version only has Stray Kids for vocals and its done in a more Live format so you can really hear their voices. 

From what I heard, most of them found this song really challenging because of the focus on vocals and it being in English and worked with coaches to prepare. I think they did such a good job. I would recommend checking out the official video after, just because its as top-tier, beautifully made as all their official videos are. 

https://youtu.be/SE05iCw3qY8?si=9BcjTTxhNykrmpdl

From left to right in the video, their names and a note about roles they're often associated with:
Lee Know - dancer
Han - all rounder with a focus on rapping
I.N. - vocals, youngest/maknae
Felix - we all know felix by now :) deep voice, dancer
Bang Chan aka Chris - songwriter/producer, leader
Hyunjin - dancer
Seungmin - main vocals
Changbin - rapper (but he has some really nice vocals in this)

structured.name: Savallion
structured.text: I agree about wanting Tim to experience Inside in order. For selfish reasons I would go so far as to suggest watching the entire thing at once 😂 The experience was clearly crafted to be cohesive since the songs really build up the emotion as they go - there are also what I think are called musical motifs introduced by both Content and Comedy that are reused in later songs that hit so good.

Leaving this here again, hehe.
If you want to expand your repertoire even more,  I recommend a singer from my country, Chile! 

Mon Laferte is a Chilean-Mexican national treasure, and 'Tu Falta de Querer' is a heartbreak song that I think would be fun to analyze. Her voice is powerful and dynamic, and the lyrics are hard-hitting, overall just a spectacular first performance to get to know her. https://youtu.be/5R1RGl4WQP8?si=28VIcSURcYSPcjRY

structured.name: Ryan Jones
structured.text:
Final Fantasy 14 (FFXIV) has an OST that is simply incredible for a game. It's one of the best parts of the game to be sure.

This is a song that is used for a couple of the group raids that goes way harder than any boss fight song has a right to! It's a very heavy rock vibe and if you end up giving it a listen I hope it inspires you to check out some more from FFXIV. This one's a crowd pleaser but there's some truly beautiful songs in the game.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=I4O5CVvwgFI&list=

He died in 2021.

Based on the 1944 song by Bing Crosby and The Andrews Sisters
Written by Cole Porter & Robert Fletcher for a 1934 movie that was never made.

structured.name: Winnie Neely
structured.text: Tim, I really like your reactions but I've noticed that no one else has spoken about something I've picked up on: Dimash's hands. Watch his hands and you'll notice that every time he's about to change vocal colour/tone he changes the hand he's using to hold the mic. At one point, in the Story of One Sky (Almaty concert footage) he's embracing the Mic in both hands almost in a prayer pose as he moves vocally between colours/tones. It's during the vocal runs/indigenous intonnations portion of the

structured.text: https://youtu.be/SMonb2lj5RE?si=JkmFes3VVqS-Qq02 I took German back in high school, don’t remember much anymore, but one assignment my teacher would have us do is translate songs because he believed music to be on of the fastest ways to learn a language. This song Leb Deine Traume by Luxuslarm was one of them. The band name translates roughly into luxury noise, and the band used to only actively make music half the year and the other half would be spent teaching music at their own school

SONG REQUESTS 10/2025:
Tim Welch Vocal Studio

Oct 1, 2025

Edit

Leave a comment with a link, and '❤️' others' comments to uptick them. Be respectful to each other. 🙏

Here we go! You all left so many amazing ideas last month, keep them coming! I will give it a couple days, and then start reacting to the songs in the comments that are getting the most 'hearts' and overall engagement from the community.

*DIVERSITY IS IMPORTANT TO ME.

I have no problem doing a few in the same style and artist, but if all the top comments are from the same music community, I might make some exceptions and react to some from another music genre, even if there are less 'hearts' on the recommendation.

I'm looking forward to seeing what you all put up! Don't forget the link.

And thank you as always for supporting me here on PATREON!

Cheers,

Tim

In collection

Second rec:
Bang Chan "Railway" - https://youtu.be/27rdSyt-Qt8?si=WldId3AlkIVnmDXs
This one is a solo song from Bang Chan and is VERY Halloween with some vampire/zombie imagery. Warning: there are some... goopy, crunchy effects here. It's a little gross with a horror theme, if anyone is sensitive to that. 

The vocals in this one are nice for me but its more for the story that I recommend it. It's very interesting from a story perspective - when you combine it with the live performances it creates a sort of loop (the live performance starts where the video ends, and vice versa), which is relevant to what the lyrics are saying. I think this one is also entirely in English so a bit easier to understand the meaning for those of us who don't speak Korean. 

Edit: I forgot the joke explanation, that Railway "is about trains"

Another song from Bo Burnham's Inside 

Content + Comedy— I couldn't find a good video of it on YouTube as officially there is just a still imaged video for both released and most of Inside is best experienced with the visuals (and the context of it being the 2020/2021 era) BUT the timestamp on Netflix from the special is right at about 0:45 for Content and 4:00 for Comedy. I list these together because Content is very, very short and really just serves as an opener for the special and Comedy is the next song and my official request!  

Some of the other songs are available with the original video on YouTube but I think Comedy is a good place to start, especially since its towards the start of the set! Its a personal preference on my end, but I'm a stickler for going roughly in order (at least when it comes to first and last songs, the middle doesn't matter so much) just because I feel like they help set the appropriate tones accordingly. 

I did see that someone else recommended stuff from Inside, the only reason I made a separate comment is because the song in particular is different and I didn't want to comment under their post going "yes, but actually this song instead ☝️😌" If there's a different way you'd like us to go about a same artist/album but different song suggestion just let me know so I can keep that in mind going forward! I didn't want to comment under them and snub their suggestion at all, but I dont want to clog up your comments with similar suggestions either. Sorry about that!

The Stupendium (they/them) is a nerdcore rapper that primarily makes high concept music videos about games with not-so-subtle wider reaching themes. 

I recommend 3 possible entry points:

1- Adequate Wordsmith https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=6Dm5trEo9ow&pp=ygUSYWRlcXVhdGUgd29yZHNtaXRo
A self-introduction. Pure display of skills : rapping, singing, video production.

2- The Production Line
https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=HYdOZOdjXnI&pp=ygUTdGhlIHByb2R1Y3Rpb24gbGluZQ%3D%3D
A worker's anthem within a song about Lethal Company. In collaboration with Dan Bull. Genuinely interesting vocals : harmonies, lyrics that sound like vocables, rap

3- Ad Infinitum
https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=d8RkcUiCxuE&pp=ygUMYWQgaW5maW5pdHVt
Off the deep end. A Deltarune rap that answers the question "if a video game boss attacked you with music, what would it sound like". Technically impressive, disorienting, fast flow.

Howdy! I really think you'd love the production of one of Ateez's recent albums if you wanted to do another album listen through. GOLDEN HOUR: Part 3 In Your Fantasy Version. I might not be the best at explaining this but Ateez usually does series for their albums all centering around a theme, or feeling. Golden Hour, to me, feels like well... golden hour. It's that moment between day and night where everything is lit beautifully.  And their golden hour albums, in my opinion, explore all the different possibilities that golden hour can bring both before and after golden hour. This particular edition was released in June, and since you've only listened to Guerilla, this is a TOTAL vibe shift. The first 5 songs are group songs then after that are solo songs. Each solo song feels completely different from the rest. I have not been able to stop listening since the day it came out. This is a playlist with all of the songs:
https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLxA687tYuMWgX7aphxa3Wn-8M4MA2ZgI1&si=sO1wjmOxqwxwBbfW

Pasting this here cause I put it on last month's post last night cause I didn't see this one....

Gavin Dunne is an Irishman who creates music under the name Miracle of Sound. Most of his songs are inspired by video games and other pop culture. A lot of it is metal, but though I love those, I often prefer the ones where he departs from this and does something different. He's been at it for well over a decade and this year he went on tour with a full band for the first time ever and I was lucky enough to catch one of the shows.

Here are some of my favourites of his:

Payday (inspired by The Outer Worlds) https://youtu.be/PjtwNeX5wRA?si=l1BoIPYeDOwar3mD
Liquid Nights & Disco Lights (inspired by Disco Elysium) https://youtu.be/7sAxhu04SlM?si=NCnkyrkQvonDaYqO
City of Night (inspired by Cyberpunk 2077) https://youtu.be/376DUWvQroU?si=1H1T630qPIKyoUDy
Deep Blue (inspired by Subnautica) https://youtu.be/dUHp7N8BVyo?si=DoUkSOwFYyHaADiV
My Shooting Star (inspired by Firefly) https://youtu.be/KX_tb8-lYLQ?si=_6SrNYBLJNHEEPj3
Gráinne Mhaol, Queen of Pirates (Irish pirate metal) https://youtu.be/mzfq5BJFON0?si=cfjN59CgBy9tz3l6

I'll stop there or I'll be at it all night. I really admire this man and the sheer force of his creativity and his voice.

 For something more fun and, i would say a little bit shocking haha i recommend "Dalabengba" a Vocaloid cover that he sung for a talent show(i think). The song tells the story of a hero who has to rescue the princess from a dragon, a little cliche i know but the thing is that the characters had names that are actually tongue twisters and Zhou Shen plays ALL of the characters including narrator, making a total of 5 different voices, really impressive!!  https://youtu.be/XHedu3KcPP0?si=L54_yrhYiF5Mn0p2

As for something more mystical and calm, i would said this collaboration with Juan Diego Flórez, a wonderful peruvian tenor, would be the perfect choice! The video is from the spring festival gala of 2025 and Zhou Shen sang with Juan Diego Flórez famous song "El condor pasa" and the traditional Chinese song "Lan hua hua". A truly beautiful and breathtaking performance from two wonderful artists!!
